persona: This customer prioritizes product effectiveness, especially leak prevention and suitability for sensitive skin, and values functional design features like front cups and gentle ingredients; they are highly influenced by value for money, trust in performance, and recommendations from others, often purchasing in bulk and gifting the products.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. These are the only diapers my son does not leak through at night. I have an extremely heavy nighttime wetter - he's 20 months old and already wearing size 5 diapers. These are the only diapers that even have a chance of keeping him dry overnight! Review 2: rating 1.0. Leaked the first night. Leaked the second night. ... Leaked the first night. Leaked the second night. Leaked the third night. Ridiculous. Review 3: rating 1.0. It leaks badly on the sides It leaks badly on the sides (16 lb baby, diaper is size 5, rated over 27 lb) even when the diaper still has half of its absorbency capacity. It doesn't matter if you tight more/less the diaper, it leaks either way. Review 4: rating 1.0. Didn’t work for us I have s 25 lb son and these diapers leak every night . Review 5: rating 1.0. They ruined a good thing We've been buying these for a long time but they suddenly changed the product. The new design leaks every night! Hmmm. The one thing I need a diaper to do is hold pee and these just can't do it. I see lots of similar reviews from others on the new design leaking. What a huge disappointment-- you blew it Huggies -- now we need to find another brand. Review 6: rating 1.0. ... think these are a counterfeit product and they are terrible. The fakes have a very light print with ... I think these are a counterfeit product and they are terrible. The fakes have a very light print with Mickey mouse. The real ones, which are great, have a Winnie the pooh design. I buy mine at Target now because I can't trust the ones available on Amazon any more. Review 7: rating 1.0. New design is horrible. New design is horrible. WTF!!! My child soaks through EVeRY night in these and all our sleep is disrupted! Somebody needs to arrange a march for the old huggies back! I also don't see the diaper pants anymore!!!! Huggies, you need new management or something, these decisions are not for the benefit of customers.
I have pictured the old design on right and new design on left. Old design has more shape and elastic to help stay secure, new design has less el... Review 8: rating 2.0. Just Another Marketing Ploy... This diaper is the same as the Huggies Little Movers that you can get in bulk at Costco for cheaper than these nighttime diapers. The diapers have just been repackaged but provide no extra absorption or protection. I feel duped by another marketing ploy. I am pretty disappointed with them because I have been looking for a nighttime solution for my little boy. It seems like the search goes on... Review 9: rating 5.0. Great night diaper! I have been using these diapers for bedtime for my little one for about 3 weeks now and they have been wonderful. I have to say I have never been a fan of Huggies, but I am definitely a fan of Huggies OverNites. I used to be completely faithful to Pampers and I used their Baby Dry for bedtime since my daughter was born (she is on size 5 now). That was of course until Pampers decided to change their Baby Dry diapers to be lower quality, which made me start looki... Review 10: rating 1.0. Malfunction? Many of the diapers are separating. Disappointed. Review 11: rating 1.0. What happened to this diaper? This is such a disappointment. Huggies had the #1 overnight diaper for a reason. I'm not sure why they would change this. I have been buying Huggies Overnights for the past several years using them on both my sons. I was surprised to get them in the mail and see the look was completely different and the diaper was much thinner. But I still thought they would work just as great as the previous diaper. I was so wrong. My son (who never ever ever leaked in ... Review 12: rating 1.0. These diapers leak horribly! I have been using luvs These diapers leak horribly! I have been using luvs, which sometime have leakage overnight on super wet nights, but these leaked 10x worse than luvs. Very disappointed. Review 13: rating 2.0. Two Stars These were no better than regular daytime diapers. Review 14: rating 5.0. Keeps the bed dry My youngest has been peeing through his diapers since he was a newborn. The kid is a fountain. These are the only thing keeping me from changing the sheets multiple times a day and my son from waking up during the night. We're out right now and using regular Huggies, and it has been a mess. Highly recommend for heavy wetters.

I did NOT receive this product free or at a discount in exchange for a review. I paid the listed price. This the reason there mayb... Review 15: rating 5.0. There is a noticeable difference. Much thinner and far less effective. Don't waste your money. I'm very frustrated and disappointed. Review 17: rating 1.0. Don't buy!! They fall apart! These diapers work about as well as the Pampers Baby Dry Exrta Protection diapers is used to buy for my son before Pampers decided to switch the night time diapers to being Swaddlers (which was a huge mistake because they hardly work for all night protection AT ALL). The thing that I don't like about the Huggies diapers is that they stink so bad even just half way through the night. The Pampers brand night time diapers never smelled bad even when they... Review 20: rating 5.0. Better than Pampers night for us; dry mornings. We've had much better luck with these than Pampers night diapers. Twin boys, 18 months, both sleep about 12 hours a night (miraculous in its own right), apparently both are half bladder/half baby on the inside based on the amount of milk and water they take in at dinner. With the Pampers, every morning they and the bed were either wet or soaked. We've seen great improvement with the Huggies, such that they'll wake up either dry or with ...
reference_output: rating 5.0. Would recommend These are awesome and prevent leaks

persona: This customer prioritizes functionality, safety, and practicality in baby and toddler products, with a strong preference for items that are easy to use, well-fitted, and reliable; they value thoughtful design that enhances everyday caregiving but are highly sensitive to quality degradation and usability flaws, often switching brands when performance declines.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Not good for ikea twin bed without box spring Not good for ikea twin bed without box spring. There’s no room for mattress to fit securely on top of bed platform with this under. Review 2: rating 5.0. Works Great Perfect for when our 2 and 4 year old grandkids visit. Easy to assemble - read the directions. Folds well enough to store in our attic without disassembling. Review 3: rating 5.0. Reduces Worry About Falling Off I put these on a queen sized bed because of co-sleeping with a baby/toddler. The bed is pretty high and these greatly reduce concerns about the baby falling off. I would like to have been able to buy some that are a bit longer but just like this. We centered these on the sides and use pillows to reduce/block gap near headboard and foot. Because of the length really 4 1/2 stars. Review 4: rating 5.0. Have to use them together I gave it 5 stars because the product itself is fine. But I was expecting to be able to use these separately, one on my son’s bed, one on my daughters bed. But apparently they are only able to be used together. So, that’s my fault for misunderstanding, but I wanted others to know that in case they thought the same thing. Over all, a good bed rail for kids. Review 5: rating 5.0. Great! I purchased this thing to prevent my little old Shih Tzu from rolling off our Queen bed! She's fallen (rolled) off our rather high bed three times before we invested in this thing. Her falling was completely frightening to us, and to her. She sleeps at the end of our bed on top of the covers. So now I can sleep in peace knowing she's safe.

If you're thinking about purchasing this thing, be aware that you need to CLOSELY FOLLOW the instructions for putting the thing ... Review 6: rating 5.0. Well made-safe for toddler. Hard to follow directions-I had to disassemble and re-assemble to get it right. We haven't used the side fold down feature so I can't really comment on that. Good news: our 20 month grandson was so proud of his "big boy " bed that he led all our visitors and his parents into his bedroom at our house by hand! We practiced before bedtime getting into and out of the bed and he came out for breakfast the next day beaming. This was placed on the upper half of a... Review 7: rating 5.0. Fits great on a double bed Fits great on a double bed. I feel comfortable with it, and confident my 2.5 year old won't fall out of bed. The drop side is nice, but the rails are pretty tall, so it doesn't fold completely flat against the bed. The bed I have this on is VERY tall too, so I was surprised by this. It has a full bed frame under the mattress as well as a standard size box spring. Review 8: rating 1.0. Missing piece so we can’t set it up This product probably would’ve been great. Videos and pictures all seem great however, we were sent a damaged box that’s missing a piece.

I just wanted to update that I contacted the manufacturer and was able to get the entire thing replaced for a new one with correct pieces and it was great! Review 9: rating 2.0. Rail did not fit sleeve One rail assembled ok and fit into the cover but the other side did not fit into the cover and these are connected to each other so you can’t really just use one rail. So disappointed I would have ordered the regular single rails had I know. Review 10: rating 5.0. Double bedrail Perfect for the double bed. Bed is open on both sides so the double bed rail works perfect. Review 11: rating 2.0. Didnt Work Terrible design. We have a platform bed that has a lip around the mattress and the parts of this wouldn't fit. We ended up buying a foam pillow roll that you put under the sheets works perfectly for safety and was easy. easy to return. Review 12: rating 5.0. Works great on full size bed These are perfect for a full size bed. When I cranked it down tight, it makes it almost impossible to use the feature to fold down the one hinged side, so we never do that. Its not a problem though, because it's still easy enough to change the sheets on the bed with this still on. The best part of having these on my toddlers bed is that she can store her water bottle, books, and stiffed animals in the space between the bed and the railing so she can easi... Review 13: rating 2.0. Incomplete The part that suppose to engage one side with the other didn’t have the screw that secure and make the “click” sound that let you know it’s engage Review 14: rating 3.0. Three Stars Nice quality, not tall enough​ for deep mattresses Review 15: rating 3.0. Good rails, but WILL NOT WORK WITHOUT BOX SPRING The rails are good and sturdy and I know my child will be safe. Although these are designed for a bed with a box spring. A mattress directly on a bed-frame with side boards will not allow the side to fold down as intended. You can see in the photo the hinge point is at the base of the elbow, so it will not work unless the bottom of the mattress is free and clear of any obstructions. Review 16: rating 2.0. Broke during build process. Got it out of box was zipping the mesh on to the rails and it broke and ripped Review 17: rating 4.0. Does the job Works well on my queen size platform bed, feel safer with baby on bed with us. Only issue is can't fold down with the platform frame, so have to scoot to end of bed to get out. Other than that, sturdy and does the job Review 18: rating 5.0. Works on a frame without a box spring. I wasn't sure if this,would work on the new frame we purchased for our toddler since it has wood slats and no box spring, but it works perfectly! Highly recommend! It's great for the price too, very sturdy. Review 19: rating 1.0. Kid flipped it and mattress on top of herself. No instructions and difficult to install alone My kid flipped this and her twin mattress on top of herself in the middle of the night. I heard nothing and then a thud over the monitor. When I went upstairs to check on the noise I found my 3 year old was trapped under her mattress. Super scary. I removed the product from her bed. Better safe than sorry. Assembly was fairly easy- once I googled instructions. They were not included. This pr... Review 20: rating 3.0. Works So Far! So far, so good! Granted I'm writing this only a week after installing it. My review is mediocre mostly because the installation isn't strong. It doesn't attach to the bed and the mattress simply sits on it, becoming part of the guards support system. This may be a flaw in the design. However, it has held up to my very active 3-year-old sleep right up against so I'm very happy we purchased it.

If I can, I'll update this review after a year of use. :)
reference_output: rating 4.0. There is a project mistake Took 3 star because the mattress itself when someone lays down press the button to release the cord that secures both parts that keep them together. I had to put something covering the button between the mattress and button to avoid this. Project mistake for sure.

Happy with my purchase... I would recommend this product. Review 17: rating 5.0. Awesome Product! I'm so excited to have these protectors for my leather seats! I love them so far. reference_output: rating 5.0. Great to Save your seats from Child Car Seat We bought these to help protect our car seats from impressions once we installed our child's car seat base. It has done the job perfectly. Would recommend and buy again.

persona: This customer prioritizes a secure, well-fitting cover that maintains its shape and integrity over time, values ease of cleaning and durability through repeated washes, and is willing to accept minor imperfections if the product performs well functionally and offers good value, but will reject items that compromise safety or fit despite positive material qualities.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Bought for the nuna sena play yard. A little ... Bought for the nuna sena play yard. A little big, but put a fitted sheet over it. Held up to machine washing well. I did air dry it though.

Review 2: rating 1.0. Poor quality. Poor quality..washed it before using and it ragged out and looked like it had been used for years. Was also too large. I do not recommend

Review 3: rating 2.0. Not waterproof; it has not the inner cover Probably it was just mine, but it came without the waterproof inner cover, therefore, I had to add an extra layer of sheet for my baby’s bassinet - pack & play. It fits very well and it was as it has to be except that mine is absolutely not as it was claimed. I bought the one for the crib and it came with the waterproof inner cover.

Review 4: rating 1.0. I’m not happy I did not receive the mattress it says delivered but I only got the pack and play.

Review 5: rating 1.0. Doesn’t Fit Way too big, doesn’t fit the Pack N Play mattress at all.

Review 6: rating 1.0. poor quality Placed in gentle wash and hung to dry before the first use and the stitching is coming apart. Its sitting in my laundry room until I can try and find someone to fix it

Review 7: rating 3.0. Doesn't fit pack n play. I ordered this for the Graco pack n play we have in our room where we plan to have our newborn sleep in the beginning. This mattress cover is a little stiff and feels like it will be durable. The cover fit a little better after being washed but it is still too big! The stretchy corners that tuck around the edges cover the velcro patches and buckles so at the play pin stage the whole "mattress" would be unsecured and slip around.

Review 8: rating 1.0. Does not fit pack n play I bought this for a Graco pack n play. The reviews on this product were great, but as soon as I opened the package I didn’t understand why there were so many good reviews. The product is stiff, rough, and is too wide for the pad for the pack n play. Because it’s too wide it doesn’t lay flat and wouldn’t be comfortable for my newborn. Also, I can tell after a few washes it will start to fall apart.

Review 9: rating 1.0. Awful Put the mattress pad in the wash before using, followed washing instructions, one wash and it is shredded, just awful. I trust items to be of good quality when I buy them, so I threw away the packaging as soon as I received it, I have no way to return it now. What a rip off.

Review 10: rating 1.0. Unusable after initial washing I washed this before using and it came out looking like it was ten years old.  It was so pilled up that I could not use it on my pack n play.

Review 11: rating 1.0. Not good quality Baby slept on it for a week and it looks terrible. Haven’t even washed it and it has completely started piling. The material feels like felt. I can’t even imagine what this would have looked like if I had washed it.

Review 12: rating 1.0. Terrible This product it didn’t fit in the pack and play mattress at all me a total waste of money very misleading

Review 13: rating 4.0. Adds padding but not a great fit I bought this to mainly add extra padding to our pack n' play since our daughter would not sleep in it. It dies add extra padding and helps get rid of the noise of her rubbing her feet on the synthetic cover. My one complaint is that it is not a smooth fit. It bulges on top and is bumpy, even after putting sheets on. I wish it was a tighter and smoother fit.

Review 14: rating 1.0. Don’t buy This ain’t water resistant, it started to shed after one use id never buy this again

Review 15: rating 3.0. Perfect fit...not exactly waterproof Very soft and comfy...there is no crinkly plastic layer. Yet waterproof is not how I would describe it. Review 16: rating 1.0. Top fabric frayed after one wash I washed this item in anticipation of my baby's arrival, but the top fabric was already fraying and coming off after one wash! My lint trap consisted of the top fabric material.

reference_output: rating 2.0. Doesn’t fit Graco Pack n Play Causes mattress in Graco Pack n Play to buckle and become unsafe. Elastic is just a a little too strong. Product seems waterproof and would be great if it fit. I have noticed pilling.

persona: This customer values well-made, effective noise-blocking products that perform reliably in real-world scenarios, especially during travel and household activities, and is willing to continue using the product long-term if it delivers consistent performance in specific situations.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Baby earmuffs I bought these for my sister-in-laws baby shower. I was hoping for the baby to use them less than 3mon of age, which is the suggested age to begin wearing them. They will be perfect since they shoot guns, etc. However I think they will work just fine in October at our clay pidgeon shoot! The ear pieces and head band are soft leather material, and are adjustable to many lengths. Review 2: rating 4.0. Very Happy :) My husband and I purchased this product so that we could take our 6 week old son to a college football game (my husband is a die hard fan and we have season tickets) He was able to sleep through most of the game and there were rarely any instances where we noticed that a noise startled him! We were very impressed :) The only reason that I am not giving these 5 stars is because I found that they were a little difficult to keep on his head ... true he WAS only 6 weeks bu... Review 3: rating 5.0. Great hearing protection for baby Our 9 month old wore the earmuffs for an entire football game. The home team won so it was loud, but she was still able to take her nap Review 4: rating 5.0. Great for Flying Bought this product because I'm a music fan and will love to take my little boy to concerts. I used it for our first trip to the beach and was an excellent help with noise in the airplane. He slept the entire flight. Looking forward to use it in the next trip. Review 5: rating 5.0. Sons loves them! Our son has been able to nap when we've taken him to Disneyland and now on his first plane trip, thanks to these headphones. One man who was sitting behind us on the plane was wondering what magical sounds we were playing on the headphones that enabled our son to be so content and well-behaved. When we told him they were just for noise reduction he seemed very impressed. Your plane neighbors will thank you! Review 6: rating 5.0. muffs These are good muffs for your baby! I use ours so my 5 mo son can be out while we are shooting and firing our cannon. He would not be able to be around all this without the hearing protection with them on he is as happy as can be. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great product, let's us enjoy NBA and College Hoops Our primary use case is sporting events NBA, College Hoops on a biweekly basis. We have used since our baby was 1mo. He is 4mo at the time of this review.

There is some concern about putting these on a baby under 3mo due to their soft skull. While this product cannot official recommend putting it on a baby under this age, we found the tension to be very light. One can reduce it further by stretching out the headband o... Review 8: rating 5.0. Perfect for our LIttle Hockey Fan We have used these twice at live hockey games, and will continue to use them. I'm sure that with out these ear goggles our little 6 month old would have been very upset during the game. He even falls asleep in them, so I know they are comfortable. They expand enough that they fit comfortably on my head too, so I'm not worried that he'll grow out of them anytime soon. Highly recommend! Review 9: rating 5.0. Great! I bought these for my 13 month old son to wear to a skeet shoot. At first he wouldn't wear them but we worked with him for a couple weeks and now he loves them and pulls them out for us to put on him! My son had never been around gunfire and first thing at the skeet shoot was firing off a cannon!! I put the muffs on him and he didn't even flinch! (but I did!) Great quality product! Definitely recommend! Review 10: rating 5.0. Great! I bought this for my infant son because we were attending a fireworks show post-baseball game when he was 3 months old. They worked really well for that occasion and for several other events with loud music.

After hearing how much damage can be caused to infant's ears, I definitely would recommend using these for any event that has a constant loud noise. Also, they are completely adorable. Review 11: rating 5.0. Great protection for baby's ears We bought these in preparatation for the 4th of July and our baby boy. We haven't been able to test them on him yet, but they fit him perfectly and actually expand enough to fit our own heads. We tried them on and they do seem to block sounds out very well. I would recommend this product. Review 12: rating 4.0. Not bad... They are very inexpensive, relatively well constructed, and a decent level of protection. Well padded for highest level of comfort relatively wide range of adjust-ability. Review 13: rating 5.0. Worked Perfectly Bought these for my little one (16 weeks) for when we go target shooting at our personal outdoor range- they worked amazingly well! The noise did't seem to bother him at all. I tried them on myself and they seemed to suppress the noise very well. I highly recommend! (I won't be missing any more concerts either!) Review 14: rating 5.0. Great for 18 month old Purchased to take our 18 month old to a concert. They were comfortable enough for him to keep on for long periods of time, and seemed to cancel out enough noise. Perfect fit on a small setting, and will grow with him. We did not have to use the smallest setting, so this could work for a younger child, too. Review 15: rating 5.0. This made our international flight (>12h) so much better. Baby (5m) slept peacefully through some mild turbulence etc. Also other children and or babies did not wake him up. On an earlier flight one baby would start crying making another baby cry, then another... Review 16: rating 1.0. Broke the second time we used them We took our daughter to two football games. The second time we went to put them on her the plastic connector on the ear broke. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect Product I LOVE simple products that do exactly what they need to do without adding a bunch of gimmicky junk. This is one of those products. We bought them because we were heading to Jazzfest with our newborn last spring and they were perfect. We never got too close to the music, but even from a distance it can be tough on a baby's ears, but not with these. She didn't mind wearing them at all and as a bonus, virtually everyone who saw her commented on how cute they looked. Review 18: rating 5.0. Perfect! These earmuffs fit our 7 month old son perfectly. He doesn't mind them being on at all. We go to a lot of loud events (snowmobiles, loud trucks etc) and he wears them without any issues. He hates wearing hats but will wear these without complaint. Review 19: rating 5.0. Rodeo, car show, mowing lawn, and home renovation We bought this when our son was 9 months old for a fair and rodeo. We were in the stands and it was noisy, something that would usually bother him but he wasn't bothered with these on. He sat on grandpas lap the entire time watching the rodeo, just fascinated. Afterwards we walked around the fair, I kept them on him since the fair was noisy. He never pulled them off. I like how they are padded on the ears as well as the portion along ... Review 20: rating 3.0. baby ear phone Bought these for our boston terrier so she could go Airboating with us I am having to alter them But I think they will work.
reference_output: rating 4.0. well made, but baby takes them off, depending on activities... We ordered this for my daughter when she was 7.5 months old for her to wear when we flew to the Mainland from Hawaii.  I used it at home & she slept through me vacuuming without fuss, but on the plane kept taking these off.  They fit well and block sound well.  I still use them (she's 10 months old now) when I wear her in her baby bjorn carrier & vacuum.  For some reason, she doesn't take them off then.  Great product - not sure how to prevent kids from taking the off if they don't want to wear them.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Review 19: rating 5.0. I love this oil for pregnancy! I tried lots of different creams and oils before finding this one. reference_output: rating 2.0. Large pump amount, too much. The spout shoots out oil every time with so much force your bound to get splashes everywhere. It’s so frustrating I never use mine.

persona: This customer values practicality, ease of use, and effective mess management in baby products, prioritizing items that enhance convenience during diaper changes and sleep, and are easy to clean and store.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Wish I'd bought it sooner! We were traveling to multiple destinations by car with a 12mo and wanted to avoid rental costs at each locations for a crib, so we bought this mattress to improve the comfort in our Graco Pack n Play. It worked! Our baby didn't fuss and it was more supportive than the pack n play's pad. Size wise, I understand what other reviewers were saying about a small gap when used within a Graco pack n play. Even with the small gap, I would have preferred this added m... Review 2: rating 5.0. Sleeping like a baby I was SO nervous about buying this to put in the pack and play go...you know, because they tell you never to use other mattresses. Well I’m SO glad I did! Baby girl sleeps so much better, it’s firm enough for her (she’s 5 mo). Fits perfectly with no gaps (measure your play yard). Regular pack n play sheets fit too! Review 3: rating 1.0. Not very comfortable!!! The mattress fit the pac and play perfectly but it added no cushion or softness to make it more comfortable for sleeping. Review 4: rating 4.0. This mattress has been a life-saver! Fits our standard Pack and Play and works with Pack and Play size/mini-crib size sheets.

It seemed to help our 9 month old sleep in the pack n play, as we were having trouble transitioning him from a rock n play. So if you have a really young infant it's probably the best way to go as I did extensive research and no one else makes a ma... Review 16: rating 5.0. Safe and comfy! It is firm—which makes it safe—but still WAY more comfortable than the thin pad that usually comes in pack n plays. I like that it is easy to clean if there are ever accidents on it. reference_output: rating 5.0. Great for pack n play sleep Got this for my son's pack and play (babytrend) and it fits perfect! My boy sleeps better with this than the flat hard mat that comes in it. Definitely would recommend.

reference_output:
rating 1.0. Plastic leash slips off easily -- UNSAFE PRODUCT THIS IS NOT A SAFE PRODUCT!!! I'd like to give this 0 stars. The latch on the leash came off when my son was in the parking lot and I had luggage I was struggling to get in to car (6mo pregnant, on a west to east coast trip by myself with 23mo son -- really needed an extra pair of hands or a leash that WORKED). The problem is that the latch is cheap plastic, not even as nice as the metal pull down latch on pet leashes. Because of that, its prone to getting tangled in the plastic anchor that is on the chest band and slipping off, especially if you have an active child running around with it for a while. My son was running circles around me in the car rental store, we went to the car and I was putting luggage in when the leash slipped out and he ran right out from between cars into the parking lot while I was screaming and dropped luggage and nearly tripped trying to get him. Thankfully no cars were coming through but it was a busy lot and he could have been hit. TERRIBLE. They should put a higher quality latch on the leash part, one that does not so easily slip off.

Also, this is NOT an easy product to get on a toddler or to adjust to the size. I agree with others, the toddler in the photo has a turtle neck on, otherwise her neck would be rubbed raw. The straps do not adjust in enough places to help it fit well. The velcro is really hard to get it to line up well so that it velcros enough and is secure, snaps or a loop to loop it through and then velcro to secure would have been better.

My overall take is that this is a good product that took too many short cuts, too cheap, made too many bad decisions for cheap construction that ultimately render it frustrating and unsafe to use. I will probably just sew my own, to my liking and fit, for my toddler -- and it will be safer and better.

This is the best piece of equipment I own for my 5 month old. She's too young for a high chair so this is perfect for her. She loves being in it. We put it right on top of the kitchen table. It's durable, light weight, and fabric is machine washable. I highly recommend this for babies ages 3 mos until they can sit up on their own, and even after.. !! Review 3: rating 5.0. Loved it! This thing! I loved it! Pretty much saved me until my baby grew out of it! I used it when I needed to jump in the shower or do the dishes. Review 8: rating 5.0. WONDERFUL Best best best invention ever. I had my 2 month old twins with limited neck control sitting up in this chair checking out the world. She loves it! I was looking for something besides her bouncer and tummy time to place her while I work in the kitchen. This is perfect now that she is 2 months old, and the price was right. In this seat, she can sit up and look around. I liked it so much, that I bought a second one to take to her day care! Review 11: rating 1.0. One Star Tight squeeze for a boy baby. Review 12: rating 1.0. Don't love it probably shouldn't have purchased this Don't love it probably shouldn't have purchased this. He'll sit in it for up to 15 min at a time. Once he's a bit older I'm sure enjoy the toys more. It's nice and soft and supports well. My only complaint is that there's no way he can squeak the squeakers in the monkey feet. They're in a really weird place and they are more under his legs than under his feet. Review 14: rating 5.0. My 4 month old loves this chair My saving grace! My son cannot yet sit up on his own but refuses to recline. Basically he makes me hold him upright all day. He doesn't really like his swing or bouncy chair anymore now that he's a supported sitter.
This chair makes us both happy. He gets to sit upright like a big boy and my arms get a break! Review 15: rating 3.0. Tiny As other reviewers have noted this seat is small!! But works well for reflux babies who need to sit up after eating! Review 16: rating 5.0. great for reflux babies Love it! Havent heard issues with hip development with this seat like the supported moulded chairs have. My little one loves it! Since he has reflux and has to be upright a lot... It helps keep him upright so I can do some things around the house. There does need to be some head/ neck strength on the babies end. The monkey face is not a real support... More a floppy piece of fabric that is just for decoration. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect for my 9 weeks old boy Worth paying extra. Bought it for my 9-week-old boy since his neck was pretty strong and he started rolling over at 8 week olds. He stayed in it a couple of time a day (happily). He was always happy. Review 18: rating 5.0. The seat is easy to get in and out of and is very ... My 2 1/2 month old granddaughter loves to sit in the seat and watch her big sister play. The seat is easy to get in and out of and is very stable. It folds easily for storage. Review 19: rating 3.0. got it for my six month old it was hard to get him in and out and as he grew we could not use it ,so i paid 45.99 for one month maybe less of use Review 20: rating 4.0. SUCH a better buy than a Bumbo seat SUCH a better buy than a Bumbo seat! Both of my kids NEVER could manage to get any use out of the bumbo; they were either too little for it, or too big/strong.
This keeps little babies secure, but has a nice wide base, so bigger babies dont tip it over (aka, like the bumbo!))

persona: This customer prioritizes functional performance and durability, with little tolerance for poor image quality, limited screen size, or inadequate battery life, and is unlikely to repurchase products that fail to meet basic usability standards.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. MBP41 Motorola Video Baby Monitor - High Quality! [[VIDEOID:44dd8213c2a311e7e0118a1c85a2a568]] This is an excellent, high quality baby monitor. I bought it last week. It's my first monitor ever and it's a great one. The video shows how I got it up and running and shows some great features as well. The best features are the intercom, camera panning, zooming in and out, night vision, 2.8 inch screen, and the fact that the remote control buttons are next to the screen (I really didn't w... Review 2: rating 5.0. High quality video monitor - best I've ever owned! These baby monitors are incredible! The Motorola MBP41 Remote Wireless Video Baby Monitors are made with high quality construction. The video image is crystal clear and the ability to zoom, pan, and tilt make this unit super convenient, especially if your baby moves around a bit, or you want to get a closer view of your sleeping child. The parent unit has a stand, and the camera and parent units both have rubber feet. I love the rech... Review 3: rating 2.0. Spotty reception and bad charging connection Thought I really liked this monitor but after about a month of use the charger connection is loose so in the middle of the night if it's just a fraction loose it's starts beeping because the battery is dying! When you're already waking multiple times a night this is the last thing you need. Also the signal is spotty and the sound goes in and out constantly even though we are right in the room next door. Also wish the volume was easier to t... Review 4: rating 3.0. Three Stars Nice product, but the monitor doesn't stay charged for more than one night or day. Review 5: rating 5.0. This is an exceptional monitor. I used it for ... This is an exceptional monitor. I used it for one month while caring for my husband during his last days of life. It is so sensitive you can even see when an individual's eyes are open when the room is dark or when breathing is irregular. Exceptional value. Review 6: rating 2.0. Good reception, no sound Help! I just started using this. I've used it 3 times so far. The monitors reception is great! Picture is good. But I just noticed it sounds like the volume is working, but it is NOT. I have it turned up all the way under the settings menu. If I wouldn't have waked right by the room I wouldn't have known the baby was crying. Anything else I could try? Review 7: rating 5.0. Love it! This monitor is everything I was looking for! Love it! Review 8: rating 1.0. This monitor is terribly constructed. We were given the monitor as a ... This monitor is terribly constructed. We were given the monitor as a gift and very quickly the power switch got stuck in an inverted position. We bought a second one thinking that was a fluke, and the second one didn't work right out of the box! The screen was black. I will never purchase a Motorola product again. Review 9: rating 5.0. Awesome Product AS described, works great, have no complaints, use it everyday!!!! Review 10: rating 3.0. Three Stars Does the job but speaker stopped working properly, sounds distorted. I guess its not a deal breaker. Review 11: rating 2.0. This worked fine the first three times we used it This worked fine the first three times we used it. The fourth and so on, we keep getting "No Signal" errors. We can be within 30 ft with a clear line of sight and it will go from full service to no signal at random. It will beep every few seconds when there is no signal which is a great safety feature but it's frustrating being woke up constantly throughout the night because of it. Review 12: rating 5.0. Love this monitor Love this monitor. It's a great price, the picture is very good, and I love that you can move the camera back and forth. Review 13: rating 3.0. No improvement from previous "older" models. I bought this product (MBP 41 2.8") hoping it would be an "improved" version of my previously owned Motorola MBP 36 PU (3.5" screen). Unfortunately it is not!
The unit works fine "out-of-the-box" and is easy to use. Picture quality is ok even with the smaller screen and microphone works well. After all, this is not a "retina" screen :)...For that I give it 4 stars...but there are still a few issues with... Review 14: rating 1.0. worked well until it didn't work at all This monitor worked great. Then, after less than 3 months of use, the sound stopped working completely. Motorola is sending a replacement, but they will not send it until they receive our old unit in the mail, leaving us completely without a monitor while we wait (we had been using the video without sound). Review 15: rating 5.0. Best Monitor Out There By far, the best monitor I have ever had. Not only can you see your child on the screen, you can push buttons to move the camera back and forth. The second feature that is amazing is the intercom. The baby/child can be in another room and you can push a button on the monitor and talk to them directly. I would highly suggest this monitor to anyone having children! Review 16: rating 1.0. One Star Never will buy this model again. It came damaged. Review 17: rating 5.0. A very good product I am so happy with this! Seriously. Buy it if you are looking for a video baby monitor. We had it for over 3 months now and I could not be happier. It has a good range, charge is enough for 7 hours - which is what I need when I am working at home. Of course you will need to keep it plugged in for the night to ensure that it lasts. The quality of the image is decent and I like the zoom in feature - I can even see my baby breathe.
I love that the camera tilts. ... Review 18: rating 5.0. Five Stars This is a great video monitor for a great price!! Review 19: rating 1.0. The camera is good, however the battery life is terrible The camera is good, however the battery life is terrible. For example, I take it fully charged with me while I'm taking a shower that way I don't have to rush thinking that my baby is crying by the time that I get to the room and start to look for something to put on it only has a bar, so the battery basically lasts about 40 mins at most. Review 20: rating 5.0. Great monitor! No problems with this monitor. Works perfectly fine. Night vision great and it's nice to be able to move the camera from the base unit. Definitely recommend this one.
reference_output: rating 1.0. Average to bad Really bad image, small screen, and the battery lasts less than an hour when not plugged in to the wall.

persona: This customer prioritizes product functionality and reliability for infants and young children, especially in items related to feeding, safety, and ease of use; they value sturdy construction, effective design, and compatibility with existing equipment, but are sensitive to usability flaws such as poor flow, instability, or discomfort during extended use.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. Strikingly disappointing for the money I really wish I could go back in time and tell myself not to buy this.

I dropped $70 on this, when money was tight right before my first baby was born. But I thought, this will save my sanity, because all these people who reviewed it on here say that it will. Not so.

At first, it was thrilling to use whatever bottle we wanted to use. The Kozii warms them all! We use Doctor Browns and Emfamil and those warmed up nicely. Howe... Review 2: rating 5.0. I love kiinde products I love kiinde products. everything is affordable and awesome. get the gift set and it comes with everything you need for $100 including the bags, adapters and the bottle warmer. Review 3: rating 1.0. Don't buy if you only need to occasionally warm bottle On our second Kozii. Customer service was very helpful and sent us a replacement when our first warmer stopped working. This is not a warmer for people who use it occasionally. It stopped working every time we were gone for a long weekend. Within a month our second one stopped functioning as well. To clarify we also followed instructions for filling, used only filtered water, even covered the top so things couldn't fall in, and ... Review 4: rating 1.0. Very disappointed This item stopped working after 1 month of usage. It does not boil water anymore and the only thing that works is the timer and water does not boil. Very disappointed with the product. Review 5: rating 5.0. GREAT! No thinking needed, no wasting of water! My baby is eating breast milk exclusively, but for a few reasons I've been pumping then feeding him via bottles most of the time. The Kiinde Kozii is a little spendy, but in my opinion, well worth it. There's no thinking required, and it wastes no water, compared to the water you'd need to run to warm up a bottle. And it's much faster than just sitting a bottle in a bowl. Works great with bottles and with frozen bags. And since it's so ... Review 6: rating 2.0. Knob breaks We have owned two of these and my son is only 10 months old. Both broke in about 3 months. The heating portion still works, but the timer knob breaks. It gets stuck at a certain time so you have to shut it off manually. Both did the same thing. It heats slowly too. Sometimes 12 minutes for a 6 oz bottle of cold formula. I've had warmers that did this in 5 or so. Pretty inconvenient in the middle of the night. Review 7: rating 5.0. ... used this bottle warmer for five months now and love it. It is fast We've used this bottle warmer for five months now and love it. It is fast, easy to use and efficient. Much easier than standing at the sink wasting water. Our third child was born in June with a heart defect, so I couldn't breast feed him. I've been pumping and freezing the milk since then. So I've mostly used the bottle warmer for frozen milk. I would defrost anywhere from two to four frozen ounces at a time. To... Review 8: rating 5.0. Best Bottle warmer on the market! YOU MUST BUY THIS ITEM. You will not be disappointed. If it is one thing that we have learned with baby items it is that you get what you pay for! We tried 2 other well rated bottle warmers and within a week we were fighting with overheated bottles of hard earned pumped breastmilk and a screaming baby waiting to be fed! This is amazing water swirling technology that heats the bottle PERFECTLY everytime. heats bags of frozen breastmilk as well as ... Review 9: rating 4.0. its ok This bottle warmer has a good idea. I was tired of being burned by steam from other warmers so using hot water was a great alternative. The only problem is that if the milk/formula level is above the water level it doesn't get warm and you have to add more time. Other than that i like the product. The bottles are usually the perfect temp, never too hot, which is a problem i encountered with steam warmers. My daughter has learned to be patient since it does take longer! Review 10: rating 5.0. love it so much i have two love it so much i have two!! I received munchkin warmer as a gift but it got so hot it boiled the milk!! this warmer not only warms the milk ok, but doesn't get the bottle hot. its great. wish they made a travel one! Review 11: rating 5.0. Great for Breastfeeding/Pumping Moms! I tried 3 different warmers...one didn't fit my bottles, one didn't shut off automatically, & all of them basically boiled my breast milk which ruins the nutrients in the milk. This is the only one that does not overheat the milk. I love it! I sometimes use my Ventaire Bottles from the fridge (just have to hold bottle upside down to get the water out that collects at the bottom, which happens when you warm Ventaire bottles), I sometimes use Me... Review 12: rating 5.0. Only one I'd ever use for breastmilk Awesome warmer. Only one recommended by my Dr for warming breastmilk. So glad I purchased this and learned about the importance of not overheating milk. Review 13: rating 5.0. does its job I have been using this for about two months now. It works great as indicated. Sometimes, you need to adjust the time because it does not fully warm the milk perfectly when you follow the instructions. But you will get used to it since it is related to your fridge condition or room temperature. Review 14: rating 1.0. Pump issue Loved the product but after having it exactly 6 months the pump has stopped working. Going to contact the manufacturer to see how well they stand behind their product. I'll update with the results.

After exchanging a few emails with the manufacturer we were sent a replacement unit that worked for 5 months. Love the product but not very durable. Review 15: rating 4.0. We bought this as a replacement to our original. ... We bought this as a replacement to our original. You must keep on top of descaling. Ours lasted 10 months before it finally died. Review 16: rating 4.0. Perfect for frozen breastmilk I needed something to defrost all of my breastmilk bags while I travel and my husband is keeping our daughter. This was very easy for him to use and we feel the heating temperature is very safe and will not scald her. We strictly use it for breastmilk bags and at times it takes 12-13 minutes to defrost. My only other issue is if the breastmilk bags start leaking the milk escapes into the water and will need to be cleaned out. Review 17: rating 1.0. Waste of money Horrible! this takes forever to get the bottle to a good temp, huge waste of money! Review 18: rating 2.0. Not worth it. Stopped working after 6 months. Only made it 6 months and it stopped working. The cleaning process is also ridiculous and time consuming. A bowl of hot water works better and easier. Review 19: rating 1.0. A Waste of Time and Money I would not repurchase or recommend the purchase of this bottle warmer. It failed to consistently warm both breastmilk and formula in Playtex drop in bottles. In some instances, the water simply would not rise to warm the bottle despite adherence to the directions. Don't waste your time and money. Review 20: rating 5.0. Best for Breastmilk I went back to work when my son was 4 months old and my husband stays home with him. I wanted to find a warmer that made feeding as simple as possible (our little guy has quite an appetite and usually requires some, um, distraction while the bottle is warming). But I was also wary of warmers because everything I read about breastmilk seemed to caution how easy it was to destroy all the great stuff by overheating. So I was really happy to discover this one. It was ...
reference_output: rating 5.0. So far so good! I've used it a handful of times and ... So far so good! I've used it a handful of times and it's worked well. The instructions are simple. The only weird thing about it is having to keep the water level within a very narrow range. It's a little trickier when you wake up in the middle of the night and are half asleep.

persona: This customer prioritizes natural, clean ingredients and effective functionality, especially for sensitive needs like infant digestion, and values organization and ease of use in baby products and accessories, while being willing to overlook minor usability flaws if core benefits are met.

I would recommend sticking with Desitin as it doesn't... Review 5: rating 2.0. Disappointed I love Burt's Bees items but this was disappointing. The consistency was a little clumpy but the main thing was I really couldn't stand the smell. The travel sized one didn't seem to smell so strong. THE BEST Diaper Ointment I've tried! We've tried a number of diaper ointments on our baby, including Desitin, Eucerin, Boudreaux's Butt Paste, Vaseline, plain old coconut oil, and even a prescription anti-fungal creme-- Burt's Bees is the by far the best one of the bunch! First, my baby's diaper rash cleared up within a day to a day and a half. It forms a very effective moisture barrier on my baby's bottom, it's such a good barrier that it even remains after I change a pee diaper a... Review 7: rating 5.0. Lovely diaper rash cream I love this stuff. I alternate between this and Triple Paste when my toddler has diaper rash.
Be aware that it's NOT good for cloth diapers, so I use a liner (or a disposable if I have to).
I like the smell of this so much (I'm a sucker for lavender). It works great for us, but like I said, cloth diapers beware! Review 8: rating 2.0. no healing component I love the smell but, No Zc in it. MY Pediatricien recommended me not to use i in case of redness as there is no healing component. I was surprised but I follow her recommendations. Without redness, I could use one way more cheaper and that doesn't leave your hands white. Review 9: rating 5.0. cloth diaper safe Love it, works wonderful, and smells good too, and is good for cloth diapers. Not much else to say :) Review 10: rating 3.0. I don't like the white color of the cream and the smell ... The texture is okay and soft. It is natural ingredient. However, I don't like the white color of the cream and the smell is not very nice. I prefer something translucent. Review 11: rating 1.0. It doesn't have any effect I used this product when my daughter had Diapers rash but it didn't effect and her rash was getting worse then someone told me about destine,but it didn't work as well. Finally I decide to put nothing and open effected area for few hours and her rash go away with in two days. Review 12: rating 5.0. Works Great! With the birth of our first child, we were looking for an all natural product line and a friend recommended Burts Bees Baby. I've ordered and used everything (bath oil, bath soap, lotion, diaper rash ointment, multipurpose ointment, etc.) and we have been highly impressed. Luckily I caught him before he could have hurt himself with it as he was starting to run away.

persona: This customer prioritizes functionality and compatibility with existing baby gear, values product reliability and ease of use, and is willing to pay a premium for well-designed, purpose-built items that solve specific parenting challenges, especially when they enhance safety and convenience.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Fabulous Changing Pad! I wanted a nice thick changing pad and this met all of my expectations and more! I couldn't believe how much more expensive they were at other Baby Stores. This one was so perfect we actually bought a second to have one upstairs and one downstairs. The size is big which we love, even though our little one is pretty little, she is secure with the straps and contoured sides! Great product and even better price!

Review 2: rating 5.0. Excellent 2nd Changing Pad We bought this as a second changing pad to keep downstairs. My son is 2 years old, and we change him on the floor using this pad. I love that it is longer than the pad on his old changing table, since he's too big for his changing table now. I can squeeze our regular changing pad covers on it but it's tight. My son flops down on it without hurting himself because it's so thick and sturdy. It does the job!

Review 3: rating 4.0. Works well, but watch the straps We bought this changing pad for use with a newborn.  As other reviewers noted it is nice and thick, and is a good size that allowed us to continue using it as he grew older.  On the plus side, the raised edges provide a little extra comfort level for the parent, and also help keep anything unwanted from getting off of the pad (if you have a boy).  On the negative side, as another reviewer noted, the straps can scratch the surface underneath the pad.  ...

Review 4: rating 5.0. perfect this is exactly what we needed.  something durable, safe and practical.  it even comes w/ screws to secure the safetey belt into the table or dresser.

Review 5: rating 5.0. Great Product! Bigger and longer than I expected it to be, but it's not a problem. It will allow me to use it until my baby is even two years old.  I like that it has non-slip bottom, which will be great since we're putting this pad on the top of our baby's dresser.  I don't think this really even needs a pad cover - you can just wipe it off, and it's not rough plastic.  It's rather soft, actually.  I'm happy with this purchase!

Review 6: rating 3.0. Good for the price Before my second child comes in September, I knew I wanted to get a new changing pad. I decided to go with a contoured changing pad with raised sided for extra protection. The Summer Infant Contoured Changing Pad seemed to have everything I needed for a great price. It is very thick and comfortable, had plenty of straps to attach to the changing table and to the baby, and the raised sides offer reassurance about the safety of the item. The only thing I didn't like ...

Review 7: rating 2.0. Dissapointed. I was so disappointed when I received this changing pad. It had good reviews and the price was fair. I have a feeling I received a bad one, but have no way of knowing that for sure.  The vinyl on mine was twisted/contorted and wouldn't lay flat down on the pad. I even tried to adjust it a bit, but nothing helped. Had to return this item.

Review 8: rating 5.0. Gets the job done! This pad was a great deal and so far it gets the job done! It fits with all standard changing pad covers and secures the baby nicely.

Review 9: rating 4.0. Nice changing pad I like the size an cushion of this changing pad.  It is a standard size, so most covers will fit nicely on it.  I put this on top of our dresser, and skipped the separate changing table to save room in the nursery.  My only gripe about this model is that it only has one security strap, I have seen others that have two straps.  But this isn't that big of a deal, as the underside of the pad is a non-slip material so it helps to keep it in place.

Review 10: rating 2.0. Review 11: rating 3.0. Great product but the price went down $5 the week after i bought it. :( Great product but too bad for me that this week the price is $5 less than it was last week.  Thanks alot!

We also got the ultra plush changing pad cover (also by Summer Infant) which is super soft, a great price, and fits perfectly.  I highly recommend both this changing pad and the ultra plush cover...

Review 16: rating 1.0. Unbelievably filmsy. Used another brand's changing pad for 10 months before it ripped. This one ripped (same baby using it) after the first use. Unbelievably flimsy.

The covering on this is so thin that it tears at the slightest pull. So disappointed. What a waste of money!

Review 19: rating 3.0. This thing is HUGE I have not had my baby yet.....but is it really a must to have such a HUGE changing pad? I assumed these things were smaller.

Review 20: rating 5.0. Great product at a great price I shopped around in stores to replace my dirty chaging table pad. the prices out there were about $30 in stores. I recommend measuring your changing table to make sure you order the right length. Is seems there are a few different lengths of table on the market and you want to make sure it will fit properly. The measurements provided for this were accurate and it fits my table perfectly.  It is good quality and a good price.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great product, gets the job done! If you are looking for a great inexpensive changing pad, get this product.  I saw some reviews complaining about the quality.  I am not sure what they expect for the price you are paying and I am not sure what you expect for a changing pad?  We just used an old dresser in our babies room and this fits perfectly on top.  We bought several changing pad covers and it looks great and works perfectly!

persona: This customer prioritizes product safety and durability, especially for use around children, and is highly sensitive to design flaws that could cause harm; they are unlikely to repurchase or recommend products with poor construction or hazardous materials.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Better than Other Brands I love this one.... quality made, product. What really made it valuable is how it comes packaged with an extra straw! I hate how quickly the Thermos and Playtex straws wear out, and in the past have ordered replacements from the manufacturer, but the cost of that is almost not worth bothering. SkipHop has a great product and great understanding of how the product will be used, as demonstrated by including the extra straw. Review 2: rating 2.0. Leaks! I was looking for a straw bottle that doesn't leak when turned upside down for my 18 mo, and this leaked straight out of the packaging. i am seriously annoyed b/c it's not a cheap bottle, and since we opened it i can't return it anymore. no bueno! persona: This customer prioritizes timely delivery and product functionality, with a strong preference for items that work as expected upon arrival; they are willing to repurchase if the product performs reliably, but frustrated by defects that render the item unusable despite positive initial impressions.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Works well, but no drain I bought this because my daughter grew out of her infant tub. I was a little skeptical because there were no reviews, and it appears that my skepticism was well placed. So far, it's a good tub and I like the little pockets to put toys and soap, but there's no drain! Since I can't pull a plug at the end of bathtime to drain the water, I have to wait until after I take my baby out and get her dressed before I can dump the whole thing. It's a little bit heavy wi... Review 2: rating 5.0. I really like it! My baby is 6 months old and she is already enjoying it. I think it can make shower time really cool and for sure I will take it on vacations next week. Review 3: rating 2.0. Super Slippery!!!!!!! This product inflated easily enough, is adorable and is easy to store (i just put a command hook in my shower and hung it up). However, it is EXTREMELY SLIPPERY. We used it last night for the first time with my 8 month old son and he was sliding everywhere. We did not have this problem with the safety first tub we'd had for our older son. I will have to buy a bath mat to put at the bottom of the tub and will need to be even more alert that I was previously, ... Review 4: rating 1.0. Too slippery! First of all this tub is a nightmare to inflate, I blew it up by mouth and it took forever and was very awkward. Next filling it up with water was difficult because the side came out too far from the spout and I ended up having to use a cup to fill it with water. When I put my son in it I immediately realized how slippery the bottom was and I could barely keep him sitting up. Every time he would start moving around he would slip from one side to another and flip over on... Review 5: rating 2.0. Pops holes too easily! It would be great if it wouldn't pop a hole and leak after only a few weeks of use! All of these blow up tubs do this. Review 6: rating 2.0. Too small! This tub is way to small for a toddler...too bad b/c it's adorable. Better off ordering the one from "One Step Ahead." Review 7: rating 2.0. Not what expected Not quite what I expected. On the small side and shredded upon inflating it. Had to return for a refund.
Not interested in ordering another one. Review 8: rating 3.0. Nice for traveling, but baby slides around in it a lot. I always bring this with me when we travel with our son because you never know what products were used to clean other peoples/ hotel room bathtubs. It's definitely useful for traveling, but you better keep a constant hold on your baby because their little butts slide all around on the flat bottom of the inflatable and can whack their head on the sides of the bathtub. I don't use it at home for that reason Review 9: rating 3.0. Some reservations I purchased this inflatable tub when our previous one started deflating constantly. This one is cute (with Elmo and Cookie Monster) and seems to be of good quality. It fits into our standard tub, and our baby enjoys bathing in it. My only complaint is that the pillow takes up a lot of room and is not removable, so our baby will grow out of it quicker than tubs without a pillow. Our baby is currently 15 months old and about 32" tall. She fits in it fine for now.... Review 10: rating 4.0. Good for Travel I purchased this inflatable tub for travel. My family is constantly on the move, my four month old has already stayed in 3 hotels! I read through other reviews before purchasing because overall it was a great item.
After using it I would definitely say its a must have.

Pros
The cubbies/pockets were great for holding the shampoo and wash cloth while bathing.
Deflates quickly for travel.
Suction on rear was great to hang dry in the hotel... Review 11: rating 5.0. Strong Material and Baby Loves it!!! I've used another of these tubs for over 2 years and just got this replacement, after the first one developed a slow leak. My 3 year old loves it and the bottom of the tub stays cushy and filled with air for years, which is amazing, because the baby is "Really Rough" on it. Purchase rounded bath toys for this item, as "anything" with even a "slight" edge or point will end up puncturing it when the baby's "exuberant... Review 13: rating 5.0. Great product and safe for babies Great bath tub my son loves it ...!!! rating 1.0. You have no other option The cup was a bit difficult to install, but I eventually got it in. reference_output: rating 5.0. ... believer in trying different bottles to find what is best for your baby I am a firm believer in trying different bottles to find what is best for your baby. Going to try for stage two soon. Overall, huge fan and have not dealt with any gas issues.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Great monitor Currently using both cameras, on one screen & it works like a charm. Highly recommend this monitor if you want to use both cameras/2 separate rooms. Review 2: rating 4.0. Great features...Ok camera We started with an older, simpler Summer Infant camera and loved it but needed the second camera and the great features of talking through the camera and a moving camera. Those features are great, especially if you have a large house and a mobile toddler. However, the image on the camera is nothing compared to the older model. My biggest complaint, though, is that unlike other Summer Infant monitors (including the one we ... Review 7: rating 5.0. Grandmas best purchase ever! Pros:
1) The big screen is nice when viewing one room. And when the room is lit up the picture quality is great.

3). The ability to pan around the room is a... Review 20: rating 5.0. Awesome capability for worry free parenting. Item arrived on time, in right packaging, as ordered. I just can’t believe once again the price difference.

The sound from the speakers in the camera is more then I imagined, and the sound back to monitor from our daughter is spot on, the sound quality literally hear...
reference_output: rating 2.0. Avoid this monitor. Over-priced for poor quality and not-so-great features... The sound quality is horrible. Also the video off feature that I assume is supposed to help with battery life is just annoying. What's the point of a video monitor if you can't see any video? I understand doing this at night time when you're sleeping, but I sometimes rely more on the video than the sound when I'm watching her and doing something where I can't hear the monitor (i.e. blow drying my hair). The sound mutes when you pan/tilt which is just annoying even if it's only for a few seconds (and the pan/tilt is very slow which is also annoying). I don't like that there is no sound when in split screen mode using both cameras. This monitor is just lacking a lot of features I need to make it fully functional for our setup and preferences. I would NOT recommend anyone getting this monitor.

persona: This customer prioritizes functionality and reliability in baby and parenting products, favoring items that perform well in real-life use cases such as safety barriers and absorbent, soft cloths, while being willing to purchase additional units based on proven performance, though they are critical of products that fail to meet specific practical needs.
reviews:

My grandson loves it and so do I, he is 20 months old and the lock is secure, it keeps him entertained, it is easy to assemble, lightweight but strong folds up easily, and is a space saver. Putting it together was fairly simple once I realized how to read the instructions. Its super sturdy and I only gave easy to fold a 3 because i haven't figured out how to do it yet without taking it all apart.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Sturdy, cute baby gate We actually use this as sort of a fence in front of our extensive shelving to keep our toddler from pulling everything out and climbing the shelves, and a few panels to block items in our entertainment center. It works great.

persona: This customer prioritizes functionality and durability in shower accessories, and is likely to avoid products with poor performance or reliability, even if they are low-cost or widely available.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Shower head eventually falls out It worked great for a month or two, but the shower head eventually started falling out and bonking me on the head. While the suction part seems to work great, the cradle that holds the shower head is made of VERY soft plastic that distends over time if the shower head has any weight at all. Maybe this would work for a very light shower head, but mine is apparently too heavy. Review 2: rating 2.0. not quite what I expected Unfortunately, the shower head falls off this repeatedly. It was purchased in hopes of making showering easier to not have to reach so high for the detachable hose we have in our shower. For just resting the shower head, it's fine. But once the water is turned on, it takes on a life of it's own twisting and turning and spraying water outside the tub and consistently falling off, resulting in sore feet when it crashes unexpectedly. Review 3: rating 5.0. great for kids This product has saved me from helping with showers! We had a stall shower that the kids were using. The shower head was so high that the kids were having a hard time rinsing. This product works great and holds tight. We have placed this about 4 ft. high and the kids can easily reach it and clean themselves and replace the shower head. Worth every cent. We haven't had any trouble with it falling off of the wall or the shower head coming out of the holder. Great ... Review 4: rating 1.0. One Star kept detaching from wall wouldn't hold the shower head Review 5: rating 3.0. Doesn't seem to want to stay up I bought this with a hand held shower head to bathe my 1 year old son with. The showerhead is great, but this little "Mommy's Helper" is anything but. We have tried attaching it in several different spots in the shower and it just doesn't stay stuck. We know how to do it, it just doesn't seem to hold it's suction for some reason. We will keep trying, hoping that maybe it's just a bad surface, but honestly, I'm not impressed. Review 6: rating 3.0. USE LIGHT AND SMALL SHOWER HEAD ONLY Suction cup works very good. It never comes off the wall accidentally. However, white plastic holder for shower head is very thin and weak (somewhat flexible). I have a big Waterpik shower head and this item doesn't hold it tightly, resulting in slip off the holder in 10 mins. Review 7: rating 1.0. Holder not secure Shower head kept falling off from the slot (to weaks holder). After I been use it for a month this product just not strong enough to hold a medium shower head (small head should be ok) Review 8: rating 5.0. 5 year old boys as a rule hate taking showers. If you manage to get them into the shower, they do one of two things. They either scream "Mommy!" or "Daddy!" at the top of their lungs until you come help them grab the shower head (and they don't stop until you do), or if they've seen spider man on TV recently they decide to climb the walls of the shower and attempt to grab the shower head t... Review 9: rating 2.0. Quality doesn't last I purchased the Mommy's Helper Safer Grip Portable Shower Arm from Amazon.com. This item worked very well for about 20 days. As was mentioned in another review, the plastic grip that holds the shower head stretched and now will not hold the shower head. The product addresses a need but unfortunately, the manufacturer did not invest in strong/hard plastic and as a result, the product does not work beyond a couple of weeks. Unless your shower head is weightless,... Review 10: rating 5.0. Durable I bought this Shower Arm grip to help my aging mother. She has a shower chair that she uses when she bathes so I thought that I would get her this so that she could just attach her shower head hose close by. I set it up for her and the suction cup is very strong and the arm stays in place. Review 11: rating 4.0. Works Great ... just pops off wall once in a while It's adjustable, easy to mount, flexible and works with either of my shower heads. Once in a while, though, I hear a "crash" in the night ... and sure enough, in the morning it has popped off the wall and is lying in the bottom of the shower. But not often enough to make me consider replacing this holder. Review 12: rating 1.0. My crying niece says this item is terrible My poor niece had the shower head fall smack on her head because of this thing! There she is, happy as can be and then SMACK! I should have tested it out more before I let her play around this thing. Attached to SMOOTH tile while it was dry. The second the bathroom got a little foggy from the warm water down went the shower head. This thing was worthless. I will DEFINITELY be returning this and getting my niece a toy to make up for her brui... Review 13: rating 2.0. It kept falling off the wall Unless you have very smooth tile wall, it will easily fall off the wall. It does not work on my travertine tiles. Review 14: rating 4.0. Companion to shower spray Needed: a holder for a shower spray that could be reached by a seated bather. This worked perfectly. Suction mount that can attach to larger bath tiles, or to the side wall of the tub. It did not slip or fall off, and swivels to accommodate most needs. Review 15: rating 2.0. Portable shower arm was advertised to stay in place I chose this product to hold the shower head at the side of the tub for sitting in a chair. After about 3 days it fell off the wall. Luckily no baby was underneath it and the tub was not chipped by the falling shower head. Review 16: rating 2.0. Doesn't hold very well. The suction on this holder is not very strong. It appears to stick very well to my fiberglass tub walls, but falls without warning when you put the shower head on it. This could be a hazard it you are using it to bath a child or a puppy. Review 18: rating 2.0. Flimsy My son loves taking a shower and feeling like one of the guys. He is disabled and very small for his age so we thought this could help him shower by having the shower head at his level. Unfortunately this keeps falling off the tile multiple times during a shower. I gave it two stars because I really like the idea of the product and am not sure if my tile may be more porous than usual? Other auctions cup/type items seem to work fine.

Bottom line - I would love this p... Review 19: rating 2.0. Two Stars It needs to be on a very flat surface and after awhile it does fall off Review 20: rating 2.0. Doesn't hold shower arm! I have been wanting one of these for quite some time. It seemed like the perfect item to help my shower loving kids on their way to showering themselves. Unfortuntealy, it does not hold any shower head. We bought 3 different shower heads and all popped out. I looked in Home Depot, Lowes, Target and Wal-Mart... not one store carried a shower arm lean enough to stay put. All of the shower heads we purchased would just fall out, because the tops were too heavy a...
reference_output: rating 2.0. Suction doesn't stay to long and falls, thus dropping ... Suction doesn't stay to long and falls, thus dropping the shower head. After about 100 falls, the shower head broke.

persona: This customer prioritizes product effectiveness and safety for their baby, especially in preventing issues like diaper rash and skin irritation, and values practicality and ease of cleaning; they are willing to pay a premium for trusted, high-performing essentials but are disappointed by poor durability, missing components, or unclear functionality, and tend to favor items with vibrant colors, thoughtful design features, and proven reliability across multiple uses.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Trusty Baby Butt Ointment Trusty old A & D ointment always works for keeping my baby's hind quarters protected against the massive amounts of waste she produces. As expected, it creates a protective barrier between her baby butt cheeks and her frequent pees and deuces. This size is much more economical, and lasts longer than the smaller size tubes. More importantly, with the giant A & D ointment tub, you get to use every last precious golden drop of the butt protection without the fo... Review 2: rating 5.0. My go-to for my children I've favored this over many other brands and ingredients. It's non-complicated by ingredients list and a sufficient amount in each use will ensure that there will be no diaper rashes (three years and none thus far).

PSA: not friendly to heat and may melt and harden; store in a cool place. Review 3: rating 2.0. NO SEAL Was not sealed??? No seal on the product when I opened the tub. Perfect. I've been going to a flotation spa where you float/soak in epsom salt. The salt was wreaking havoc with tender membranes and little cuts and the petroleum jelly the spa was providing was simply not effective in creating a barrier, so I purchased this to take my own. Works perfectly - I don't feel any sting whatsoever from the salt in tender areas. Review 6: rating 1.0. One Star I bought 05 units and only received 03 how can we resilver it? Review 7: rating 5.0. By he way no returns on this guess the seller knows, how crummy it is
Guess it's OK for babies they can't complain

I have never written a negative review, even if a product did not live up to my expectations, but felt people need to be warned Review 9: rating 5.0. Best cream out there Absolutely love this cream. That speaks volumes because my children all have sensitive skin. I buy one every other month. Review 13: rating 2.0. Smells bad Smells really sharp and disturbing. I can even smell it while I am kissing my baby's face. I think it is more concentrated from the tubes. I don't recommend. Maybe it can be use for healing for a period but not for daily use. Review 14: rating 5.0. Just slap it on after every diaper cleaning! This is amazing stuff. As a new parent we somehow let a diaper rash occur and people were like don't use baby power just put on some protectant so the skin doesn't get raw or bothered and so we started using petroleum jelly and then Aquaphor because I love that stuff but it's expensive and harder to grab and slap on. This A+D tub has the same active ingredients plus another one that heals and makes it even better for way less than Aquaphor... Review 15: rating 1.0. Does not include Vit A &D.
