Amazon would not return, even though when I searched, I entered VITAMIN A & D ointment and this showed. Amazon improve your search engine Review 16: rating 5.0. Best Baby Skin Protectant Best skin protectant I know I use this religiously on my baby button and testes at each and ever changing. Diaper rash is no joke so i like to make sure he is well protected. I put a generous amount not too much, little past his eump for those "surprise poops you get every now and then. The tub was full to the brim and it last me aprixinately 1.5 mos, i subscribed as its much more convenient. You will deff be using alot of those so totally worth it. It i... Review 17: rating 5.0. Not just for diaper area Used this on my baby to prevent diaper rash, but it she also got rash a few days after I purchase. I decide to use just a tiny bit on her forehead and back ,less than two days completely gone. Just be warned as a strong smell, as all A&d I know . Good product. Review 18: rating 5.0. Súper buena Esta crema para mi hija le funcionó súper bien, Review 19: rating 5.0. Best ointment. It may not smell like flowers but ... Best ointment. It may not smell like flowers but it does a great job in preventing diaper rash and in odd instances when diaper rash occurs (i.e. during teeething), it heals it very quickly. Review 20: rating 5.0. Great value We’ve been using this on our little one since we brought her home from the hospital. We experimented with other diaper creams but this one was always the winner. Love that it comes in a tub, much more cost efficient than the tubes. Though the tubes are great for the diaper bag.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Haven’t had a diaper rash since we stayed using it My son got really bad diaper rashes when he was a newborn. Now I use this in the morning and at night and my sun hasn’t gotten one since we started using it. This stuff is magic

persona: This customer prioritizes product consistency and accuracy in sizing and functionality, with a strong expectation for reliable quality and correct specifications, and is likely to discontinue use or repeat purchases if these expectations are not met.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Really like this but have already had to replace my first ... Really like this but have already had to replace my first after the plastic on the bottom cracked- exposing the battery. If not for that I would give it 5 stars. Review 2: rating 3.0. Works, but screen leaks Works great for determining the temperature, and baby likes to play with it. However, the screen has started to allow water in after only a few uses so it is getting foggy and hard to see. I will be sending this back. Review 3: rating 4.0. Takes a couple tries to get it right this is a really cute little thermometer. The little screen lights up and it is color-coded to show you the temp range, i.e. blue for too cold, green for just right and red for too hot. the little screen is pretty easy to read and it has a suction cup so you can just stick it to the tub when you're done, this is especially good if you have too much stuff on the sides of your tub/shelves. My only issue with it is that I feel like it needs a coup... Review 4: rating 4.0. Easy to use and accurate. Fun design. This thermometer is accurate, we checked it side by side with a kitchen thermometer. We love the aesthetic and the simplicity of just tossing it in while filling the bath. It stores easily on the wall with the suction cup. I also love that it is BPA and Phalate free, because of course it sometimes doubles as a bath toy. I would have given it five stars but the back light only flashes on and off with the color and does not remain on. I wished ... Review 5: rating 1.0. Not very efficient but better than nothing UPDATE 6/23/2015: This thermometer stopped working after almost a month so I exchanged it for a new one thinking I had one that was defective. After receiving the replacement, it stopped working after only 1 week of use so I returned that for a full refund. Good thing I've drawn enough baths that I know what the right temperature is for my baby and no longer need a thermometer.

***************

The first one we ordered broke within a week (stopped reading temperature correctly and would flash different colors when in the water). We sent it back for a replacement, which was an easy hassle free process but the replacement then broke within 2 weeks. This time, the light just would not stop flashing for several days straight until the battery wore out.... Review 8: rating 1.0. Less precise than "meh it's not burning me". This thing is terrible. The screen is tiny and it's hard to read temperature when it turns off the screen - which it does for some unknown reason. The thermostat button came broken, but my husband fix it. And 4 months later it doesn't works anymore! Review 14: rating 1.0. Review 18: rating 5.0. Love the LED display I am a new mom and didn't know what temperature to make my little ones bath. I tried the $3 rubber ducky that is suppose to tell you if it's too hot and it was terrible. I bought the whale thermometer and it works awesome!!! As soon as I put it in the bath tub it starts working to monitor the temp. I love that it flashes green to know that you have the right temp for my little one as I can't memorize what the correct one is. I also love that you can hang your wet... Review 19: rating 1.0. Don't waste your money! Can you give an item zero stars? Sure it's cute, and the concept is genius, but the bloody thing doesn't work. Like at all. The temp is always off, and the suction cup on the back is weak--making it fall off the wall randomly. Usually right after I've put the babes to bed. Awesome.

I might have considered keeping it if it even have a close reading, but, right out of the box it was faulty--by a lot. We drew the bath, checked it out with the regular t... Review 20: rating 1.0. Iys more accurate and useful for other stuff Flashes temp so fast you cant read ot. Flashes red, green, sometimes 00

Save the cash and buy an infrared thermometer... Iys more accurate and useful for other stuff too
reference_output: rating 2.0. Battery didnt last as long as it use to in this product We had one that worked for two years and was starting to need replacing. So we ordered another one and it died within one month.

persona: This customer values practicality, durability, and ease of use in products designed for active lifestyles, particularly those that solve specific functional challenges related to travel and pet or child safety, and they prioritize well-constructed, adaptable solutions that deliver consistent performance over time.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Potentially dangerous. Considering return after 5 months of use. We purchased two superyards and assembled them to gate off our livingroom area and allow for entry from two points. This product is excellent at keeping a baby in one spot or another. The gate is well built. It is easy to assemble and reconfigure.

It is also dangerous. Our boy (one year old now) can stick his arms and legs through the gaps in the slats of the fence. This may seem innocent at first, but ... Review 2: rating 1.0. Absolute garbage. Poor quality control from this company Absolute garbage. Poor quality control from this company. Gate button defective on arrival. Waste of time. Review 3: rating 5.0. Five Stars I brought it for my great grand daughter she loves it and so does her mommy Review 4: rating 4.0. Great product so far. I bought this to put around the area of my doggie door so the dogs can go in and out without running around the whole entire house. I do like that it was easy to put together and can be shaped to work just perfect around the area I am using it for. I originally was going to use it across the whole opening between my kitchen and living room, but changed my mind because the door is so tiny it was too much of a pain going through it. I thought it would be larger... Review 5: rating 5.0. LOVED this baby gate LOVED this baby gate! It's huge and versatile! We had one large combined room that served as a kitchen and dining area and we used this gate to section off the room in half. We turned the dining area into the play area. I could cook and clean and watch our son play while I was in the kitchen. Review 6: rating 4.0. Sturdy, Safe, Easy -- Just Really Small I love this product in that it is very sturdy, extremely easy to set up, and it keeps my baby safe so that I can do all those things that a person has to do in order to remain sane and keep her house from falling down around her. I can pour myself a bowl of cereal every morning, visit the bathroom when I need to, change the laundry over, even take the occasional shower without worrying that my little one will be chewing on lamp chords or tumbl... Review 7: rating 3.0. Did not remain stable in one place Didn't work for us. Kid was not even walking but was able to move it around the floor, or was able to change the shape by pulling/pushing. Had to return it Review 8: rating 4.0. Good sturdy, haven't used it as hexagon, but as a wall. Opens up. Can remove sections, but has to be even. Not Odd numbers. We have a LONG open living room and this works great. I wish the gate swung both directions. And it is a very difficult gate to open. But there's also not much competition in this area... even still works great. Review 9: rating 5.0. Excellent Pen: easy to assemble, sturdy, nothing for a dog to climb I bought this pen to replace the IRIS 8 panel containment pen. The IRIS is a great pen if you don't have a climber, but my Dachshund climbed it like a ladder. The North States pen has only vertical rails, nothing to climb. It's metal rather than plastic, so it's a bit heavier and not as easy to move around, but it seems to be a great product. Easy to assemble, including the wall mount hardware if desired. I had ... Review 10: rating 5.0. Connect two for 8'x4' baby jail! Perfect for twins! This product warrants its stellar rating. Thank goodness I did not purchase the plastic "Superyard" version! This gate has fulfilled all of my requirements: Not hideous, extremely strong/stable, spacious, not climbable, and tall. It has been a dream come true from the moment we set it up. persona: This customer prioritizes accuracy and reliability in product performance, and is likely to avoid products with inconsistent results, even if they are inexpensive.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. A must have!! Oh my goodness this is fantastic! I have a pile of thermometers that don’t work and never did. I even had to exchange one three times and it still didn’t work! This is amazing! I love how fast and accurate the reading is. Switching from forehead to ear mode is so easy, just press the mode button. It light up green while taking the temperature so that helps keep anxiety down those few seconds while waiting. If it glows red then your kiddo has a fever 😢 I wish I would hav... Review 2: rating 1.0. Cheap Plastic felt cheap and seemed inconsistent. Sent This back Review 3: rating 5.0. Fast forehead temperature readings When you have a squirmy baby needing a temp reading, a forehead thermometer is fantastic. This one works VERY fast so you can get an accurate reading on the squirmiest baby. It is colored to give an instant idea of how high a temp you or your children might have.
Even when my daughter was sleeping I could check on her without waking her. Definitely worth the peace of mind.
If you prefer, just pop off the top and you can get a fast accu... Review 4: rating 1.0. Inconsistent readings I was excited to get this for our baby that is due in a few weeks. We tested it out and it said we both had a fever of 102 and higher in our ears, but 97 on our foreheads. It also wasn’t even close to what our regular under tongue thermometer read. I had trouble removing the cover that goes over the ear reader as well. Review 5: rating 1.0. Doesn’t work... This thermometer doesn’t read accurately at all. I was running a little bit of a fever last night and tried to use it to take my temp and it read 97 on the forehead and 100, then 101 then 103 in my ear. These were back to back readings. I went and got a normal digital thermometer and my temp was 99.5 which was about what I thought it was. Returning this thing. Review 6: rating 5.0. Love this! Love this! It is soooo convenient I ordered it because I have two sick kids 2 and 4 and this is so much more convenient then your traditional thermometers! If you don’t have one yet you need one! You don’t have to upset your already miserable child to get an accurate temp! I do find that the ear is the most accurate than the forehead! Review 7: rating 5.0. I love how this thermometer is quick to get a reading I love how this thermometer is quick to get a reading. So hard to have little ones sit still for very long so this one works great. It's nice you can use on forehead or in the ear. Review 8: rating 3.0. Not consistently accurate I wanted to love this as my daughter doesn’t mind using this thermometer, but it is not accurate in that the readings are variably different, when taken only seconds apart. If I am doing something “wrong”, then it is too complicated to use. Review 9: rating 5.0. Easy to Use Good thing about this thermometer its dual and easy to use. the results are fast.. Can be used for both Adult and kids..The color indicator is good to identify fever.. Review 10: rating 2.0. Read the instructions The product isn’t very precise, make sure to read all the instructions before using it! Review 11: rating 5.0. Baby Thermometer Just as pictured. Works right out of the box. Easy operation. Review 12: rating 1.0. Can’t trust the results! Attempted to use this thermometer for the first time, since my daughter just got all of her 2 month vaccinations. A series of forehead measurements: 95.3, then 97.2, then 96.6. All within seconds of each other using the exact same technique: measuring half and inch from the forehead per the user manual. What is the point of an unreliable thermometer? Review 13: rating 5.0. Perfect thermometer for kids! [[VIDEOID:df8032d825090b3c3bc926e5cd29f01c]] I love that it’s so quick and easy to use even with a wiggly baby! Review 14: rating 5.0. Accurate and multiple uses I bought this to use as my diaper bag thermometer and I really like it! I like that it’s both a ear and forehead scanner. I crosschecked it again two other temp scanners and it’s accurate. Review 15: rating 5.0. Easy to use and doesn't wake the baby [[VIDEOID:3249e710d953daec8876cf92b17449aa]] We love how this thermometer has 3 different settings for house, forehead, and ear. There is also a button on the side to turn the volume off, which makes it easier to not wake the baby. When a fever is detected it lights up red. This feature makes it much easier for sleepy parents at night. Review 16: rating 2.0. Not accurate The forehead portion seemed to work but for the ear temperature, it said I had a fever of 102. I tried multiple times and multiple ways. It did the same thing on my daughter. When I compared it to the Braun, there was no comparison. I returned this thermometer. Review 17: rating 1.0. Poor product I want to return this item because it is the most inaccurate thermostats for a baby anyone can purchase. Feels cheap and not good if you are using on an infant!!! Review 18: rating 5.0. Works like a charm. Love this item. It has helped a lot where the flu has hit our whole family. Easy to use with in depth instructions. The results are easy to understand with color changing backlight. I took multiple readings on different people with consistent results. It takes temps so quick which really helps on my youngest! Would purchase again. Considering getting one as a baby shower gift! Review 19: rating 5.0. Awesome product! Very accurate Awesome product! Very accurate. I love the fact that you can check your baby's temperature without waking them up since you don't need the thermometer to touch their skin. Review 20: rating 5.0. Amazing thermometer! Amazing! Quick and easy, which is key when dealing with a sick baby!
reference_output: rating 1.0. INACCURATE READINGS - save your money Inaccurate! This gave me so many different readings - not worth the money.

Overall my wife is very happy with this for our nurser... Review 19: rating 5.0. Love this dresser Love this dresser!!! So pretty once it's assembled!! Deep drawers, nothing broken upon arrival. I'm really happy with this dresser and I think the price is extremely fair. It's sturdy and big and the 6 drawers provide ample storage. The assembly took my husband 3 to 4 hours to finish but the size and number of drawers are the reason why. It wasn't difficult, just time consuming. The only issue is that when we first opened the box there were 2 small holes and scratches on the surface of the dresser. Not a big deal to us (see photo) because we can easily cove...
reference_output: rating 4.0. Extremely sturdy I'm very impressed with how sturdy this dresser is. It's very heavy and very solid. The direction are extremely clear and the hardware is very well seperated and labeled. Took me about two hours to put together solo and very pregnant. However, it did come with several scratches on the top and one side. It was very well packaged inside the box, even though the box was destroyed, so it seems to me that these scratches were already on the product before it was ever shipped. It's not worth the pain of sending it back, but that's why it only got 4/5 stars. We are very happy with the dresser.

persona: This customer prioritizes product safety and integrity upon arrival, with little tolerance for defects or damage; they expect reliable functionality from the start and are highly sensitive to quality issues that compromise safety, especially for critical uses like child restraints.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Go buy it, or give yourself a headache, whichever OK, so we had another booster for our sons (13 months old) and it's not exactly a easy transport item. So in my attempt to get a booster seat that we could bring with us (since we are finally leaving the house with the boys after 13 months) I bought this x 2. Funny thing is...it was between this seat and the one we originally bought when we ended up buying the other. Don't tell him, but my husband's choice was right, mine was wrong.
Negatives: The eating tray that clicks on top of the bottom tray pops off pretty easily. Our little guys flings it off with ease, so we don't even use it. Fortunately, the "base tray" is easy to clean and works just as well. ... Review 10: rating 5.0. portable and easy Perfect for smaller babies. My girl is 5 months old and loves to sit up and this is perect. Easy to adjust and clean Review 11: rating 5.0. It's a winner in our house We looked high and low for a small seat like this. Tried a couple others due to low price at a big box store. What a waste. Came to Amazon to see what got good reviews from folks. This one totally does the trick for us. Our 10 month old sits in it just fine, and, its nice for grammy's house, since it doesn't take up a lot of room. Review 12: rating 4.0. Decent booster for the price It does it's job and nothing more. The tray has a white top that you can take off and wash, but it comes off so easily that the baby just takes it off herself. So we don't use that part anymore. Otherwise, it does what it needs too and it's cheap. Review 13: rating 4.0. Not bad for the price! My son loves the booster,, but he easily pulled off the top tray (not the blue cover but the actual white food tray) and sent all his food flying within seconds of being in the chair (and seconds after the pic was taken lol) So now I just put the food on the main tray that sits across his lap and it works great 😊 Review 14: rating 5.0. Secure and Easy to clean This is an absolutely excellent seat for infants and toddlers. It straps to the back and bottom of the chair so it's very secure. I'm glad I chose this booster seat over a high chair. Most of the time we eat at our counter-height kitchen table, but when we have guests we eat at a standard dinner table. Because this seat straps to the chair it works with both. When used as a booster, this seat has 3 height positions. It's easy to change between them.
reference_output: rating 1.0. I do need to say... i can ... I do need to say...i can not believe what happened.. i did seat belt (Thank goodness it was in the floor) for my FIRST USE after coming to home, although i was unhappy even before using it since there was LOTS of scratches AND some oil on it :(...... and i went to bathroom and came back and guess what????!!!!!! she crawled out of it!!!!! i of course thought it was loose and she came out BUT!....... the seat belt was broken!!! ...... i think this is my first review to write in amazon...i just hope this will not happen to any other babies... i do not even want to imagine it could had been in the chair and baby hurting...just hurting.. plz plz plz be careful moms..Thanks..

Excellent, much better than OsoCozy liners We have now tried 2 liners, the Bummis Bio-Soft and OsoCozy Flushable liners, both in size large. We typically cut them in half to double our usage and they still cover ~80% of the diaper (the most critical part.) In the size department, they are nearly the same. We have also never had any skin reaction with either liner, so this seems to be a moot point.

The difference comes in the texture and reliability. I do think the OsoC... Review 3: rating 3.0. Won't Buy Them Again These were on sale when I was low on my previously used brand ([[ASIN:B003OOCT08 Real Nappies Bioliner Flushable Diaper Liners, 200 Sheets]]) and I thought I would give them a try since they were pretty well reviewed. They do the job, I suppose, but fall apart pretty quickly. This probably makes them better for my septic tank, but I like to rinse/wash and reuse the liners that don't get soiled with poo and you can't do that with these at all; they are already on ... Review 4: rating 4.0. soft, does the trick I have tried the Bumkins, Bummis, Cutie baby and the Imse Vimse liners and like these the best. I like that they come in a roll as trying to get one single liner out of a stacked box is not easy. The imse vimse's are really rough for my baby's bottom, they are hard to tear and when scrambling and juggling a squirmy 8 mth old, they usually rip.....frustrating. These are durable, soft and I have re-used them. Initially not on purpose, but some of the liners happen... Review 5: rating 5.0. Making poop a breeze? Only a mom would be happy about writing a title like that one.

Once my baby began eating solid food, things got real. I could no longer just take my prefolds or my AIO cloth diapers and toss them into the washing machine. I tried the "shake it off" technique. I tried the "dip and swish" technique. Both didn't work and were disgusting. Excrement on my hands, excrement on the toilet seat, excrement on the floor... you get the idea. On recommendation fro... Review 6: rating 5.0. Makes cleaning up poopy diapers so much easier! These have been a life saver for cleaning up our son's fuzzi bunz diapers after a day at child care. They are very thin and soft. They do bunch up a little, but it doesn't seem to bother my son. I wish I had discovered these sooner! Review 7: rating 4.0. does the job These work the best of the various liners we've tried. Yes, they can get scrunched up in wet diapers, but they do keep our diapers clean. A different name-brand liner gave my child rashes due to a more abrasive material. The bummis liners are soft and have not resulted in rashes. Review 8: rating 1.0. Did not like! We tried these liner since my little one was leaking at night and it gave her a rash. I bought both sizes, large and small.

My baby is 4months old and I'm using BumGenius diapers. The large size liner is too wide and I have to fold the liner so it doesn't peek out of the diaper. The small size liner fits just right, but with all the baby's wiggling, the liner only covers 50% of the diaper, which makes the poo leak all over the side of the l... Review 10: rating 4.0. Decent Liner I am new to cloth diapering. I've ordered this product twice now. Most of the time the liner works and can catch the solid waste so that I can then dump it and the liner straight into the toilet. Sometimes it bunches funny and it doesn't catch everything. I've learned to make sure that the liner isn't sticking out of the diaper. This will cause leaks. I just make sure to do a visual scan at the end of a diaper change and tuck it in if it's sticking out anywhere. Review 11: rating 4.0. Favorite Flushable Liner I prefer to use no liner at all, but my parents and daycare providers really like to use a liner, so I keep these around. They have never clogged my plumbing (have been using them for more than a year, though not on every diaper). They do help contain most poops, especially formed poops. They're softer than some others I've tried.

The main drawback is that they hold urine close to my daughter's skin and seem to irritate her and cause rash. Noth... Review 12: rating 2.0. too narrow and clingy I have never caught an entire BM on one of these because they are too narrow. They are exactly the width of my diapers, but they are so thin and clingy that they bunch up and stick to my baby's body when she moves. They are softer than the imse vimse liners, but what's the point if the poop gets all over the place? I'm going back to Imse Vimse because they are wider, and less flimsy. Review 13: rating 5.0. Wonderful!! I was unhappy with the first brand of liners that I used, so I was a bit skeptical of these, but decided to give them a try anyway. Boy am I glad I did!! Bummis pros:
Smaller footprint on the changing space (a consideration for us because we have limited space there)
Large enough to possibly split into 2 liners per sheet (so effectively 200 per roll instead of 100) for a smaller baby, but not for a larger one

The differences I notice... Review 17: rating 5.0. For those that despise the sprayer These are a perfect solution to the sprayer that I don't particularly care to use. They are shaped oddly, the large is big enough to cover our diapers but they are almost square, which is odd since most diapers require coverage to be rectangular. Review 19: rating 3.0. soft but these bunch up These liners are very soft but they are not my favorite. These liners just don't stay properly. Review 20: rating 2.0. These are not flushable These liners although good but are not flushable, as the seller claims. reference_output: rating 2.0. Rough on the bottom As convenient as these are, they didn't work for my little man.  The material is soft enough, but caused a serious irritation on my child's bottom.  I am not a fan of hearing my son scream when he poops.

persona: This customer prioritizes reliability, safety, and functionality in toddler products, consistently choosing items that support potty training, hygiene, and independent use while also being durable, easy to use, and effective in addressing specific challenges like leaks, picky eating, or resistance to routines. They value products with strong performance, thoughtful design, and sensory appeal—such as appealing scents or colors—that encourage engagement and repeat use, and are willing to repurchase and recommend based on proven results and long-term satisfaction.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Fantastic pull ups Fantastic pull ups! They are thick, they fit perfect and keep my son very dry for more than 12 hours.

Review 2: rating 5.0. The best nighttime diaper option out there These are the best night time diapers that we have tried and we tried a lot.

Review 3: rating 1.0. These actually cost $0.62 per count The price per count differs from the total price and is very misleading. I will be buying this product from a different source. A simple match check makes these $0.62 per diaper which is almost double of what is put in their headline.

Review 4: rating 2.0. Switched brands Switched to Easy Ups because these poke my DS on the edges and cause sores

Review 5: rating 5.0. Work well for toddler These work great for my 2 year old son for overnight.

Review 6: rating 5.0. The only thing that works! This is the only pull-up that will hold up during night-time potty training! All the other brands (even other night-time versions) leaked all over the bed. Definitely worth the cost to not have to wake up and change bed sheets and pajamas >.<

Review 7: rating 1.0. not absorbent not good quality, very thin and not absorbent

Review 8: rating 1.0. My son has been waking up every single night by pee leaking Half of the package was good the other half is completely defective, diaper isn't full but the bed is. Sleepless nights for us for days. Please reiumburse!

Review 9: rating 5.0. Work great! My son is transitioning from diapers and uses the potty during the day, but at night time he still needs protection and we wanted to give him something that functions more like underwear.  These work great, hold everything in, and last the entire night.  Although, I have ready some reviews mentioning the sides coming undone, I can assure you that we NEVER had that issue!

Review 10: rating 1.0. LO complained they were Not comfy and not absorbent These pull ups run small and we bought them for over night.  We have never had an accident in the target brand pull ups.  Two nights in a row with these and my LO complained about how uncomfortable they were.  Will not buy again

Review 11: rating 5.0. Gotta love Toy Story Mainly ordered these for my 2 year old because of Toy Story being on them but they hold liquids very well and actually last through the night unlike other brands. Will 100% buy again in the future

Review 12: rating 1.0. Poor Quality I ordered these because I wanted to get cheaper ones to save money & I have gotten these before, but thought maybe they have changed. I’m not a fan of Huggies. My 3 Year Old Son wore these to bed & woke up soaked & was upset about it. The Pampers & The Good Nights are the only ones I trust now. I will never trust Huggies again!!!!

Review 13: rating 5.0. No nighttime leakage! Very good product.  No night leakage.

Review 14: rating 5.0. No more wet sheets. We were having nighttime wet sheets daily until we switched to these.

Review 15: rating 2.0. Hard stuff inside diapers. Some of these diapers were hard. Like a board. The absorbant material inside was very solid, and not something I would wear on myself, much less my children who have sensitive skin. It would not be comfortable in the daytime, or at night. I like this brand because it absorbs everything for overnight, but I will have to pay store price if I keep getting hard diapers from Amazon.

Review 16: rating 5.0. Super absorbent Great absorbency. I don’t have to buy diapers and pull-ups anymore now that I found these. Works great for my 3.5 year old at night.

Review 17: rating 5.0. Class leader The resealable side seam is the difference maker between Huggies Pull-ups and Pampers Easy Ups. Huggies has the superior design that’s easier to open when changing and resealable if needed. We switched from Pampers to Huggies because of this.

Review 18: rating 2.0. Terribly designed. So sad I bought a giant box of these. Super upset that I bought an entire box of these. The sides have reverse velcro, and if you need to tighten them, they have these giant flaps hanging off. It encourages my son to pull them off constantly, which defeats the purpose of wearing it. It absorbs well for overnight, but so do other 12 hr diapers.  I would not recommend them.

Review 19: rating 1.0. Not as advertised! I have order from this vendor at least 5 times but on my last order I received the regular huggies not the one listed above. I could not return as I needed diapers that day and my son leaked through the diapers over night. Super disappointed. Don’t bother sending the diapers at all if they are not the actual product!!!

Review 20: rating 5.0. Good fit, no leaking through These are great. My son had been wetting through his pullups at night. With these, we dont have that issue!
reference_output: rating 5.0. Excellent for nights. Had issues with leaks from the side with OTHER brands and my toddler and I were fed up with late night leaks. This has saved us the hassle. I noticed my toddler sleeps more comfortably as well. It is a good thick material for night even can be for traveling if you need to make a road trip possibly. I only use at night as I am potty training however this morning my toddler really grabbed this pull up and wanted to wear nothing else(I normally use cloth pull ups in the morning). He said he loves it too and ours came in the buzz lightyear design.

"Spill proof" = "Leak" according to the packaging This description for these cups says "Spill Proof". However, when you read the tiny print on the packaging that comes with the cups, it reads: "Cup may leak if shaken, squeezed or held upside down."

Spill Proof, by definition, means that a container is designed to prevent spilling. "Spilling" by definition is causing or allowing to run or fall from a container, especially accidentally or wastefully.... Review 9: rating 1.0. You have to flip the lid over and use a little knife (or like me, your finger nail if they are long and strong enough) and manually separate the little perforations that let the plastic flex to get the straw inside. Then, the straw stays securely in the hole so well, that when you fill ... Review 11: rating 1.0. Pressure turns it into a geyser, buyer beware! Okay, so they seal tight. The straws fit tight. They are easy to hold, but there is one glaring flaw.
All this tightly sealed air makes an impressive geyser action when you put even the slightest pressure on the lid or cup. Beverage shoots out the straw and once this is discovered...you will never know peace again.

But got two red, one orange & one yellow lid. This company should at least bring it to people's attention that the lids won't necessarily match the cups. Not worth ... Review 13: rating 2.0. When you close the lid, it kind of creates a 'vacuum' and if the straw is in there, with one end in the liquid, the straw will act like a hose and come straight out.

However, close the lid, then insert the straw, and it solves your problem.

Love these cups! I give my daughter her green smoothies (spinach, green apples, strawberries, bananas, and flax... reference_output:
rating 5.0. Wabi baby sterilizer vs. Baby Brezza Sterilizer I own both the Baby Brezza Sterilizer and dryer and the Wabi baby Sterilizer and dryer and have used both for the past 7 months. I use the Wabi at home and the Baby Brezza at my lake home. I bought both to see which was better and figured that I could take the one I didn't like as much to the lake. I would recommend the Wabi sterilizer over the Brezza for the following reasons:

#1. The biggest problem with the Baby Brezza is that unless you are drying just bottles and nipples (and only 6 bottles, at that) you'll find yourself playing a game of stack 'um up each time you try to load this thing. I like to sterilize my pump parts each day, and there really is no good way to put them in the Brezza. I like to wash bottles/bottle parts, pacifiers, and pump parts all together at the end of the night, and so that leaves me with a lot to sterilize and dry. The Wabi utilizes a big basket that you can throw everything into, much more convenient. I feel like I can comfortably fit about 50% more items in the Wabi vs. the Brezza, and I can just throw them in the Wabi vs. having to carefully stack them in the Brezza.

#2 The lid on the Brezza can be hard to put on if you have anything hanging over the edge of the unit. With the Wabi, this is not an issue because of the bucket style. I LOAD the Wabi up with a ton of nipples, rings and pacifiers (honestly, I way overload it, I put everything I can possibly fit in there) and I don't have any problems with everything getting dry. You just can't do this with the Brezza because of the design. I have overloaded the Brezza more than once and when I take the top off, nipples, rings and pacifiers are falling all over the counter.

#3 The Wabi is more versatile - it can be used with the bucket only, or you can put the bottle insert into the bucket. There is also another insert that holds plates, silverware, etc (I have not used this yet as my baby is still young). There is a grate type insert that sits on top of the bucket to hold smaller items such as pump valves and pacifiers.

In response to 1 and 2 star reviews: I have not had ANY difficulty with my Wabi, so I can't speak to the effectiveness of customer service. However, I think that it is worth noting that you CANNOT overfill the Wabi with water. I always use the measuring cup and I put a little less than the top line in the machine. I found out the hard way that if you overfill it, it can leak onto the counter (just a small amount, however). I use this sterilizer very frequently, and it continues to run like a champ.

The one downside to the Wabi is that it is a bit more expensive, however, it is very much worth the extra money. While the baby Brezza will get the job done, if you have the extra cash, spring for the Wabi.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Excellent product, as the quality of the tape and price very good!!

Review 2: rating 2.0. does not stick!! first off buy the stuff thats pre taped.these you have to put the tape on yourself which is a pain!!hard to get off roll and it is so thin put peice on and it was flush then next day started coming unglued and looked horrible much less providing any protection especially if hit by my child.these were not as thick as i thought it was gonna be but doesnt really matter if the product doesnt stay on.contacted seller but they just kept sending generic response and item de...

Review 3: rating 1.0. 😑 No me agradó porque al momento de quitarlo...... el adecivo fue muy difícil..... más sin en cambio para mi bebé fue lo más sencillo

Review 4: rating 1.0. Ruined my furniture! Beware this tape doesn't come off your furniture.  We just tried to remove it from a few items after it was on about 5 months and it is not coming off.

Review 5: rating 5.0. Large roll of edging for great coverage More than enough on the roll to cover the edges of the brick hearth around our fireplace. Came with “bonus” pieces for door stoppers, but we haven’t used those yet. Edging and corners have been on the fireplace for over a month without falling off.

Review 6: rating 4.0. Soft, cushioning, easy to work with I like that it is super cushioning, my boy bumped into it numerous times and he was not bothered a tiny bit. Also, it is easy to cut into sections and you can shape or stick them to any parts of the tables/chairs.

Review 7: rating 1.0. Not for us Not worth it. Kids keep taking it off the furniture. Tape is not strong enough and I don't want to ruin my furniture with strong glue

Review 8: rating 5.0. The best for toddlers Lifesaver for toddlers! Bought 2 packages!

Review 9: rating 2.0. Terrible smell! I have bought several bumpers like this before. I thought this one was a great deal for the length of bumper and for the added 4 door stops! However, this one has a SUPER strong chemical smell compared to other ones I've bought! I don't like this at all. I hope the smell dissipates over time....Otherwise I don't feel comfortable having my kids play around this.

Review 10: rating 5.0. Awesome product Great product. So glad we bought it

Review 11: rating 2.0. Not nearly stick enough. It won’t stick well at all. It wanted to curl right up and that was before the toddlers got a chance to pull at it. It also sort of “shrinks up” after you cut it so make sure to cut longer pieces than you need so it will fill the length of the space.

Review 12: rating 5.0. Protect baby! My daughter likes to walk here and there at home so I used this for my coffee table. The product is very soft so it can protect my daughter if she touch the edge or corner of the coffee table. 3m glue attached in the set is pretty tight so the bumper won't fall off.  And the door stoppers are so cute!

Review 13: rating 5.0. Works very well Any length can be but to fit your need, double sided glue on the foam for adhere to the object, rather spend money to protect any hard material for kids.

Review 14: rating 1.0. Corner Guards Some of toddler took the cushion off, the adhesive did not hold on the edges of the dresser. The product is not strong to hold on the furniture...

Review 15: rating 5.0. We have it everywhere!!! Blends in nicely with our furniture and wall edges... super easy to install and has lasted well over a year and still going strong! Most importantly, has saved my toddler from serious bumps, bruises, and lacerations!

Review 16: rating 5.0. These work perfectly! Use extra adhesive or staples, though! We reinforced our with staples. Get the closest color to your corners as possible. Our neice has wanted to pull at them. The ones that match better aren't bothered because she doesn't notice them as well!

Review 17: rating 1.0. Choking hazard! I was super excited about this edge guard at first. We have a brick hearth that runs 10ft and it about 14inches high. The adhesive tape stuck to the brick wonderfully. The brown went well with the brick and I was happy the baby proofing looked as good as could be expected. First day with the protected hearth I set the baby down and started folding laundry. I noticed she was playing by the brick. About 20 min later I picked her up and she gagged. I took a look in her m...

Review 18: rating 5.0. Great look, HORRIBLE ADHESIVE!! We started out ghetto using pool noodles and gorilla tape as a protective measure on our fireplace. I saw this product and got excited. It looks WAY better than neon pool noodles, but the adhesive tape is worthless on brick. We ended up using the ole gorilla tape to get it to stay. If you don't mind a little cheap look and tape, great product.....so far.

Review 19: rating 4.0. Good if you're on a budget It's not very thick or dense compared to a similar product I bought, but it does work.  It's kind of a weird reddish-purple brown, which isn't preferable.  You get a lot of footage for the price, which is the only reason I gave it four stars instead of three.

Review 20: rating 5.0. Bought at least 3 months ago! I read so many different reviews for products like this, mixed reviews. I bought this one and really like it, it still hasn't come off of any of the tables I've put it on, my son has just started climbing onto the coffee table which has this on it, and it still doesn't budge when hes sliding over it!
reference_output: rating 1.0. Don’t understand child proofing Worked for about a week, then children will just rip it off. Very weak and hardly child proof.

persona: This customer prioritizes product durability and functionality, especially for infant feeding, and values items that support healthy eating habits in toddlers, with little tolerance for poor craftsmanship despite being willing to pay for reliable, well-designed solutions.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. snuggly! I tried another pillow in my 2 y/o's bed, and it didn't take. After reading the great reviews, decided to give this one a try and he likes it! I like that his head is a bit more elevated, because he has a stuffy nose quite a bit during the winter. This pillow is cozy and has a nice loft to it. Snuggly! Review 2: rating 1.0. It seems like a 99 cent pillow and the stuffing is thin ... Just opened it up.... talk about no support. It seems like a 99 cent pillow and the stuffing is thin and cheap. It more like a pillow for a doll vs a child. Review 3: rating 1.0. Washed it once and all the fibers inside got lumpy ... Washed it once and all the fibers inside got lumpy. Do not say its washable if it does that from the first wash. Would not buy again Review 4: rating 1.0. One Star Way smaller than I anticipated. Review 5: rating 2.0. Not the greatest.. Really am not sure what to say. It was a good pillow in the beginning, but now, my 2.5 year old says it’s uncomfortable. It became flat- even when I wash it (it doesn’t fluff up), and so small. She also moves around a lot so her head never stays on the pillow.. I wouldn’t repurchase this for my second kid. May be a good neck pillow for plane or car rides.. but not as toddler pillow. Review 6: rating 1.0. Doesn’t hold its form after 1 wash I was so excited for this pillow because of the great reviews. I am very disappointed as it doesn't hold its form, lumps up after one wash. I followed the washing instructions too! Review 7: rating 1.0. Too bulky For a toddler pillow I found this to be MUCH too fluffy/bulky. My daughter didn’t like it and prefers a much flatter pillow. Review 8: rating 5.0. Perfect size for the crib My daughter loves this pillow. We have 2. Perfect size for the crib. We waited until 26 months to introduce a pillow. Highly recommended when your little one is old enough. Review 9: rating 5.0. Great Little Pillow I completely understand this pillow is intended for a child but let me point out that it is also the perfect size for an elderly person as well. I purchased this pillow for my 80 year old mom who just could not find a comfortable pillow any where! It is just perfect and she is completely happy with it. Thank you for your emails. I am impressed to find a company that cares about its products and the customers satisfaction. Review 10: rating 2.0. but not super happy to have to do that My toddler loves this pillow, but the stitching on ends is very flimsy. Only one thread all the way. I ripped the tag off (as I normally would any pillow with no problem - it was one of those el cheap-o paper tags) and now there’s a hole where the tag is. I’ll mend it and keep it, but not super happy to have to do that! Review 11: rating 2.0. Two Stars Very uncomfortable and little one just refuses to use this pillow. Review 12: rating 4.0. Good pillow and excellent customer service After receiving a pillow that didn't meet the quality standards, I was contacted by Eric, one of Dreamtown Kids' owners. He was fast at resolving the issue by replacing the pillow and offering me a full refund. He was truly sorry and I really respect that. The new pillow is safe and cozy for my daughter to use. Thank you Dreamtown Kids for taking good care of your big and little clients! Review 13: rating 4.0. Good for adults too This small pillow works well for grownups who find regular pillows too big and thick for comfort. Its size and flexibility support the neck well. Review 14: rating 5.0. Nice little pillow I was really skeptical on how good the quality was going to be but this is actually a solid little pillow. Good quality, comes with pillow case, and keeps its shape (doesn’t flatten). Review 15: rating 4.0. Good pillow but a little overpriced So far, so good. My daughter is using this pillow over a week now and she likes it. It's small enough to get away from if she wants. It's filled to the correct level if you ask me. It's very soft.

The cover it came with is not the most amazing quality but I use it as extra protection under the covers I made from a standard pillow case. The pillow fits in a standard pillow case perfectly sideways so adjusting it was super easy!


Then I wondered if there was a child's pillow. I googled child's pillow and google went to Amazon and D... Review 20: rating 5.0. Great purchase Although the pillow is somewhat small for an 11 year old, it is a great option for traveling or around the house. I also use it toddlers on when my neck is sore; it is so nice, soft yet firm and fluffy. Very comfortable. Customer service is great and it arrived quickly.
reference_output: rating 1.0. The stitching comes undone Stitching of the pillow came undone right from the middle.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Title for my review This is a good seat. It's for my currently 1 year old son.
I bought 2 of them. One for my car and one for my wife's car.
I could have had just one seat and moved it back and forth between the 2 cars as needed.
It is possible. However it's heavy enough that it's not very easy or quick to do this.
A push button latch design (like with a car seat) would have improved things and made it easier to attach or remove.
Regarding the recline func... Review 2: rating 5.0. Great seat! This seat lasts and it has all the needed features. My wife is a cop and has been to a lot of training specifically related to child seats (yes I know that sounds boring), but it means that she knows what she is talking about when she says it's the best seat around. The seat is really easy and you can't beat the cup holder and the little pockets in the armrests that my son likes to store toys in. It's a really handy. The seat is sturdy and easily washable. We just boug... Review 3: rating 5.0. Great car seat that will last a long time! I read lots of reviews online before purchasing two of these car seats (one for each car). I have two kids, one and three years old. My son the one-year-old just outgrew the graco snugride infant seat. My daughter was still using her Britax Marathons that we bought when she outgrew the graco. We moved my son into the Britax seats, which are designed for babies up to 65 pounds. First we tried the Graco turbo booster but that was too big ... Review 4: rating 4.0. Graco Nautilus 3-in-1 Car Seat We have had this car seat for 3 years now. We purchased it when my son was 3 years old, and he still fits in it now that he is 6. We chose it for the great reviews it got, because we would be able to use the latch-system and harness for a long time (and still do), and because it was aircraft-compliant as we were going on a trip abroad at the time.
My son likes the seat because he is able to keep toys and treats in the cubby holes under the arm re... Review 5: rating 4.0. Was not sure what to expect I hate buying carseats. Its like buying jeans, its a pain, there are too many to choose, something is always wrong with them and the kids outgrow them.
When my daughter got too tall for her convertible I had to get something that would last us longer. I researched for a long time and finally came down to 2. What really sold me on this one, was the customer ratings. I scan those on Amazon all the time to hear what other moms say so after I read both ca... Review 6: rating 5.0. Fits side by side with twins, but tight. I was originally worried at the width of the Graco Nautilus seats that they would not fit in the same way our convertable car seats did. They are a little wider but our Hyundai Tucson was able to handle them. This is great because it will leave room for an adult back there for a short, and slightly squeezed, trip. We went on a long trip with some luggage strapped in beside them (drivers side rear passenger seat is open) and the luggage had no ... Review 7: rating 5.0. By far my favorite car seat. I have 2 boys, a 40lb 5 year old and a 35lb 3 year old. Both are too tall for the typical convertible seats, but there is no way I'm taking them out of the 5pt harness until I absolutely have to! This carseat is perfect! We've been using it for my older son for over a year and I am very happy with it. He's never complained about discomfort, and it's easy to install in both my Ford Explorer and my husband's Subaru Impreza (a bit tight on legroom in thi... Review 8: rating 5.0. Great car seat Bought this for my son when he was 2 and a pink one for my 3 year old daughter. I have had them for a couple of years and they are easy to adjust and easy to clean. Review 9: rating 5.0. Great for big kids My grandson is big for his age, but we still feel he needs the safety of a car seat rather than a booster seat. At 45 pounds he still has grow room in this seat. My only frustration is getting to the buckle on the seat when he is in the seat. The cup holder is great. We took him on two vacations this year traveling about 3000 miles with assurance of his comfort and safety. Review 10: rating 3.0. very hard to take apart and put back together I have had this carseat for about 6 months. My daughter who is now almost 3 1/2 threw up in this carseat last month. We had to take it apart to take off the fabric and wash it in the washer. It took one hour to put back together, and even then, I don't think we did it right, and now the straps won't extend enough to fully close. We have tried everything - we've spent hours taking it apart and putting it back together but cannot get the st... Review 11: rating 5.0. Great car seat This is a great carseat! I found it easy to assemble and install using the LATCH system. The straps are easy to adjust, and I really wanted a carseat that used the harness up to 65lbs. I bought 2--one for my van and one for my husband's car. We have both been happy so far. I would recommend this carseat to anyone needing a booster. Review 12: rating 4.0. Very nice Easy to install, easy to use, seems safe, the only but is sometimes is hard to buckle it but very nice car seat Review 13: rating 5.0. Awesome, awesome, awesome! I bought this car seat on the recommendation of a friend and car seat aficionado, and couldn't be more pleased. It is just what we have been looking for. Safe, solid, large weight-range for a 5-point harness, and I am comfortable installing it in both of our vehicles.
I am confident that my small 4 year-old will be in this for quite some time. The only reason we didn't buy one for each of our children was due to size limitations in our compact cars. Review 14: rating 1.0. sent back and went with Britax. Let me start by saying this is no way a reflection of safety. I have heard that this seat is very safe but I am simply going with ease of use. I knew I wanted a booster with a harness and I was going back and forth between this and the Britax. Both our other car seats are Britax but this was my first booster purchase and I thought I would save some money. I had heard good things about this seat. It took me some time to put it together. It felt ve... Review 15: rating 5.0. The BEST Car Seat! We have owned the Nautilus car seat for 1.5 years now... and I absolutely LOVE it.

Reasons Why We will Be getting another one for our younger daughter too:

1. Our older daughter is 4.5 and is still riding safely and comfortably in the five point harness.
2. The Sippie holder is built right into the seat. So no matter what form you use the seat in your child will always have it.
3. The hidden cubbies on the insides of the arm rest are... Review 16: rating 5.0. great toddler seat we now have two of these and they make for a great toddler seat! easy to latch and unlatch into the car. also easy to get your child in and out of. there is a little pocket under the arm rest to store toys and small books too! Review 17: rating 2.0. Horrible Adjusting System Pay the extra money & go to the Britax. It is extremely hard to pull the straps tight enough when getting the child in & out of the seat. Also, the chest plate is easily moved by the child while driving- making it unsafe. We bought these as our "extra" seats for the other car, which is the only reason I have kept them. If they are going to be your main seat- pay the extra money & get the Britax. Review 18: rating 5.0. BabySeat Flat This is a great seat in terms of safety, ease of use, and durability. Lastly, it's very durable and will last the lifetime of several children. I recommend this product without reservation. You won't be dis... Review 19: rating 4.0. I love this car seat!!! After my son got too big for his graco snugride infant car seat earlier than what we expected (he is 9 months and 23lbs and 33 inches tall), I had to search really hard and fast for a really good car seat that was not only safe but also looked nice. Finally I saw the Graco Nautilus in BRU and fell in love with it then saw it cheaper on amazon so I ordered it. I like how it eventually turns into a booster seat thus saving me money later on. I like the 5pt harn... Review 20: rating 5.0. Never buying another car seat! We have 2 of these car seats and they are awesome. My husband and I drive compact cars so space was an issue, but we chose this seat based on safety ratings, ability to convert to a booster with a 5 point harness, and affordability as well. This seat fits perfectly and my child has room to grow. It is easy to install with the LATCH system and she seems comfortable and safe. The buckle is a little difficult, but I have gotten used to it and I would ra...
reference_output:
rating 4.0. Second one I like the old straps and buckles on this better. I have 2--one was purchased a couple years ago and another purchased in 2013. I prefer the older one.

The padding for the crotch buckle always falls off on the new one, and the buckles are more difficult to buckle and release. I'm a little disappointed.

persona: This customer values functional, practical additions to existing products that enhance convenience and reduce maintenance, and is willing to accept minor design flaws in exchange for reliable performance and time-saving benefits.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Excellent quality, fits perfectly and were absolute lifesavers as ... I was hesitant as it was very difficult for me to find containers to fit my spectra. There was only 1 review at the time, but I took a chance and purchased. Excellent quality, fits perfectly and were absolute lifesavers as I went back to work and had to pump. Great purchase!

Review 2: rating 5.0. Works great with Spectra pump Showed up a day early :-). Works great with Spectra pump.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Perfect bottles for Spectra! I absolutely LOVE these bottles.  For those who have the Spectra pump know that it only comes with two bottles. After pumping and wanting to store the milk in the fridge right away, I would usually leave it in the Spectra bottles. Then if I wanted to pump again I needed the Spectra ones so I'd have to transfer milk, wash them, dry them and it would be a whole process. I bought the 9oz ones a few days after my son was born. They are good to store (combine)...

Review 4: rating 5.0. Works great. Wish it held a little more Have a spectra s2. Works great. Wish it held a little more, but really, no complaints.

Review 5: rating 1.0. CAUTION! The lids harbor mold. I have been using these with my Spectra pump for about a month, I diligently clean them and boil them for sterilization. However today I noticed bacteria/mold growing in the silicone lid liner of a few (not all) of the lids. Not safe or healthy. Going into the garbage!

Review 6: rating 5.0. Great collection bottles. I bought these wide-neck breastmilk collection bottles to go along with my Spectra S2 breast pump as my pump only came with 2 bottles. These bottles came in a nice box and partially in plastic.  These bottles fit perfectly on my pump attachment and are solidly constructed.  Probably my favorite feature of this set is the lids.  So many breastmilk collection lids have two components which I am sure have a purpose(nipple to make it bottle for one), but for wha...

Review 7: rating 5.0. fits spectra 2 machine perfectly, and my phillips avent ... fits spectra 2 machine perfectly, and my phillips avent natural bottles. Lids fit so much tighter than the collection bottles that came with my pump.

Review 8: rating 2.0. Falling Apart The seals on 3 of my bottles have fallen out of the lids.  You can pop them back in, but once they're out they come out with each wash. They are pretty worthless for transporting milk home from work without the seals, so I'll keep fishing them out.

Review 9: rating 5.0. Works with Spectra S1 breast pump. These work perfectly with the Spectra S1 pump; they are interchangeable with the collection kit that came with my pump which is what I was looking for.

Review 10: rating 5.0. good, affordable storage bottles to replace Avent or Spectra wide mouth I use these bottles with my Spectra S2 pump.  The non-cylindrical shape makes them easy to grip when wet, which is convenient when removing the tops in the sink.  The tops seem to seal tight, and I have been using them in the freezer. The scale on the side of oz/ml is easy to read.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Work perfectly with Spectra Pump. These bottles work perfectly with the Spectra II Pump. I have been using them for close to a year now with absolutely no issues. Great price and great product!

Review 12: rating 5.0. Excellent. Fit spectra 2 Excellent. Fit spectra 2. Allows me to consolidate and clean once home. So far, lids fit well with no leakage. Bottles seem to be surviving on top rack of dishwasher too. Overall, good product and I will order more as needed.

Review 13: rating 2.0. crappy bottles. I also doubt the measurements labeled on ... They don't gradually screw tighter, they are completely loose and then within the last cm of rotation it is closed, meaning they come unscrewed constantly. But even with the seal in there the lid is not sealed whatsoever. It leaks. I have lost a lot of breastmilk to these cheap...

Review 14: rating 5.0. These fit my spectra pump even better than Avent bottles These fit my spectra pump even better than Avent bottles.  These were great and the right price.  They work with the spectra bottle nipple as well.

Review 15: rating 5.0. Fits Spectra S2 These worked perfectly with my pump, I have the Spectra S2. I have been using them for several weeks now and have never had problems with leaking (unlike the collection bottles that came with the pump).

Review 16: rating 3.0. Fine These are fine but the maymom bottles are better because of the seal and the fact that the rings can hold nipples rather than just function as a storage lid

Review 17: rating 3.0. Not bad for the price I have a Medela and a Spectra and like them equally. The spectra parts aren't as easy to acquire as Medela items so I was excited to find these. Review 18: rating 5.0. Exceeds expectations. Super helpful. I searched for options for a while and decided to try these from a seller I didn't know (which I never do, but I was desperate) They came very quickly and many more bottles than I expected which made the price a real bargain.

Review 19: rating 4.0. This fits well to the spectra and does not leak ... This fits well to the spectra and does not leak when closed with the lid. Review 20: rating 5.0. Great bottles for a great price These bottles are great they fit the spectra pumps. The price for these is a million times better. I'm also happy that they cane with lids for storage.
reference_output: rating 4.0. Great bottles for Spectra pump! I got this for my Spectra pump since it only came with 2 and I was sick of constantly having to clean them.  These work perfectly, my only feedback would be that it doesn't show measurements to a full 5 oz (only to something like 4.75) even though the bottle could definitely hold 5 oz, and the measurements are only on the bottle once, so it can be difficult to see how much I've pumped while I'm still pumping. Overall, great set of extra bottles for a good price!

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Really great gift, pretty easy to use if you follow the instructions - wife loved it! I got this for my wife for her first mother's day and used it with our 4 month old. I saw a lot of reviews complaining about the quality or ease of use. There may have been some quality issues at one point (some of the reviews I saw were a few years old), but there certainly was not with my kit. While the video instructions and things I saw said to mix three mounds together to create the clay, mine came with just one package of clay and it was very moist and easy to use. It does say you can use olive oil if it seems dry, but I did not need to do that. It took me 10 minutes to really roll it out in the frame piece for it and I assume the people complaining didn't take the time to really smooth it out and went through it too quick. TAKE YOUR TIME - it's really not hard to use or difficult at all, but like anything you likely will be giving as a gift or keeping for years and years, take that extra few minutes to smooth it out and don't get frustrated. In my pic, you can see a few lines or wrinkles. I didn't really care and a few of them were a result of me pressing my sons foot and hand in there. I didn't want to attempt to smooth them out after with fears it would mess up the perfect foot and hand print I got. But you can do it if you're careful.

Once I got it smoothed out, it was very easy. I did do what a few people recommended and waited to do this until my son was sleepy/sleeping. It made a big difference. While the clay does NOT set quickly, if you do mess up any of the attempts for the hand or foot print due to your baby moving too much or being fussy, you will have to put them back down, smooth it out again, and start over. So my recommendation is to wait until your baby is sleeping. You do have to press down somewhat firmly but not really that hard. I didn't even disrupt my little guy when doing his foot or hand - and I never felt like it was hurting or would hurt him if he was awake - the consistency of the clay was perfect. And while it is safe for babies, I'd recommend (as the directions do) to have a damp cloth with some mild baby soap just to wash/rinse their hand and foot.

After that, you've done the "hard part". Now you just have to print some pictures, attach (with the double sides tape, what a great idea for them to include this) them to the included mat, and wait 3-4 days for the clay to set to put it together. It said it can take up to 4 days, in some cases 5. Mine was dried within 3 days, but I did wait the full 4 just to make sure. But it seemed to harden up after just a day or so and got much harder as the days went on. I'd absolutely wait the full 4 though because while it may feel hard to the touch, if there is still moisture in there, it could potentially mold or fog up your glass if you frame it too quickly.

A couple of final thoughts:
-The kit is SO nice - literally this company is GREAT and so thoughtful - it came in a nice box/kit with everything you could need. It had multiple mats (two colors), plastic letters to press into the clay (I crossed out my sons name and date for privacy, but the letters are very easy to use, just press firmly in), the clay, a mini roller, double sided tape (for the pictures), a nice frame with glass, very sturdy inset for the clay, and a really nice thank you card (thanking you for buying it). I love this company, they are great!

Please do yourself a favor and if you are even thinking about getting this, just get it. You will love it and it's something that's so special. I saw some people using it for pets too! I will be getting another one shortly for my dog.

Little Hippo - thank you for making such a great kit that helped me produce and commemorate something so special and timeless!

Honestly, they could probably charge 2-3 times the price they are charging for this and I'd still say it was 100% worth it. It brought my wife to tears and it's something we will have forever. I couldn't possibly recommend this more!
